Verlag der Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur, Mainz/ Wiesbaden 1950. == Notes == {{Navigationsleiste Ägyptische Kalendermonate bis zum Anfang des Neuen Reiches}} {{Ancient Egyptian Lunar Months}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Month of the Ancient Egyptian calendar]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian lunar months]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian festivals]] [[Category:Harvest festivals]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Renutet]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Lunar Months]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:pages tagged as Automatic wikipedia redirects in namespace ]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:return the see also]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] }} [[de:Renutet]] [[sn:Renutet]] [[ru:%D0%A0%D0%B5%D0%BD%D1%83%D1%82%D0%B5%D1%82]]

{{Infobox Hieroglyphen |NAME = r:n-Z7-t:t |NAME-TRANSKRIPTION = |NAME-ERKLÄRUNG = {{ill|Renenutet|de|Renenutet|12=draft}}
''Rnwtt''
''The Nourishing One'' |NAME2 = N11:Z1 S-N35B
1-nu-schemu |NAME2-TRANSKRIPTION = ''1-nw-šmw'' |NAME2-ERKLÄRUNG = ''First month of the {{ill|Season of the Harvest|lt=Shemu|de|Schemu|12=draft}} period'' |NAME3 = F13:X1-G17-G1-O34:Aa1-M17-M17:X1-M33
Wepet-em-asech-ati |NAME3-TRANSKRIPTION = ''Wpt-m-3sḫ-jtj'' |NAME3-ERKLÄRUNG = ''Beginning of the {{ill|barley|de|Gerste|12=draft}} harvest'' |IMAGE1 = Illustration Hordeum vulgare0.jpg |IMAGE1-WIDTH = 210px }} '''Renutet''' ("The Nourishing One") denoted in the {{ill|Ancient Egyptian calendar|de|Ägyptischer Kalender|12=draft}} the beginning of the harvest season and the first month of the {{ill|Season of the Harvest|lt=Shemu|de|Schemu|12=draft}} period as the "nourishing month." From the {{ill|Predynastic Egypt|de|Prädynastik (Ägypten)|12=draft}} until the end of the {{ill|Middle Kingdom of Egypt|de|Mittleres Reich|12=draft}}, Renutet, originally the ninth month of the {{ill|Sothic calendar|1=Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar|de|Mondkalender (Altes Ägypten)|1=Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar#Der ursprüngliche Mondkalender|12=draft}}, corresponded to the period from early {{ill|February|de|Februar|12=draft}} to early {{ill|March|de|März|12=draft}}. == History == {{ill|Alan Gardiner|de|Alan Gardiner|12=draft}} and {{ill|Richard Anthony Parker|de|Richard Anthony Parker|12=draft}} theorized that Renutet underwent a calendrical shift over time, eventually moving to the eighth month by the {{ill|New Kingdom of Egypt|de|Neues Reich|12=draft}}. In the {{ill|Ebers calendar|de|Ebers-Kalender|12=draft}}, dated to around '''1517 BCE''', Renutet corresponded to the third {{ill|Peret (Ancient Egypt)|de|Peret (Altes Ägypten)|12=draft}} month, spanning from '''February 14 to March 15''' ({{ill|Elephantine|de|Elephantine|12=draft}}) and '''February 19 to March 20''' ({{ill|Memphis (Egypt)|de|Memphis (Ägypten)|12=draft}}). == Significance == Renutet, identified with the goddess {{ill|Renenutet|de|Renenutet|12=draft}}, was revered as: * '''"Lady of the Granary"''' * '''"Lady of the Fertile Land"''' * '''"Lady of the Harvest Blessing"''' During the harvest, the Egyptians made offerings to her. '''Stelae found in granaries and field boundaries''' provide evidence of this cult. The '''{{ill|Renenutet Festival|de|Renenutet-Fest|12=draft}}''' was later celebrated on '''{{ill|Pa-en-Renenutet|lt=Pharmouthi|de|Pharmouthi|12=draft}} 27''', known as the '''"Day of Measuring the {{ill|barley|de|Gerste|12=draft}}."''' In the '''Theban calendar''', this corresponded to '''{{ill|Pa-en-Chonsu|de|Pa-en-Chonsu|12=draft}} 1 (Pachon 1).''' Additionally, as a '''fertility goddess''', Renenutet was credited with ensuring the '''"emergence of flax."''' Because flax was processed into linen, she also bore the titles '''"Lady of the Garments"''' and '''"Overseer of the Temple's Clothing Chamber,"''' referring to the belief that gods were clothed in '''"the garment of Renenutet."''' Records from the '''{{ill|Solar Temple of Niuserre|de|Sonnenheiligtum des Niuserre|12=draft}}''' indicate that the '''barley harvest''' took place in '''February and March.'''{{ill|Elmar Edel|de|Elmar Edel|12=draft}}: ''Zu den Inschriften auf den Jahreszeitenreliefs der „Weltkammer“ aus dem Sonnenheiligtum des Niuserre, Teil 2.'' In: ''Nachrichten der Akademie der Wissenschaften in Göttingen.'' No. 5. Vandenhoeck & Ruprecht, Göttingen 1964, p. 200. == Name Change == The '''first Shemu month''' was later renamed '''{{ill|Pa-en-Chonsu|de|Pa-en-Chonsu|12=draft}} (Pachon).''' == See Also == * {{ill|Tebtunis lunar calendar|de|Tebtunis-Mondkalender|12=draft}} == References == * {{ill|Rolf Krauss|de|Rolf Krauss|12=draft}}: ''Sothis- und Monddaten: Studien zur astronomischen und technischen Chronologie Altägyptens.'' Gerstenberg, Hildesheim 1985, {{Falsche ISBN|3-8067-8086-X}}. * {{ill|Richard Anthony Parker|de|Richard Anthony Parker|12=draft}}: ''The calendars of ancient Egypt.'' Chicago Press, Chicago 1950. * {{ill|Siegfried Schott|de|Siegfried Schott|12=draft}}: ''Altägyptische Festdaten.'' Verlag der Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur, Mainz/ Wiesbaden 1950. == Notes == {{Navigationsleiste Ägyptische Kalendermonate bis zum Anfang des Neuen Reiches}} {{Ancient Egyptian Lunar Months}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Month of the Ancient Egyptian calendar]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian lunar months]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian festivals]] [[Category:Harvest festivals]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Renutet]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Lunar Months]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:pages tagged as Automatic wikipedia redirects in namespace ]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:return the see also]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] }} [[de:Renutet]] [[sn:Renutet]] [[ru:%D0%A0%D0%B5%D0%BD%D1%83%D1%82%D0%B5%D1%82]] {{Egyptian Calendar Navbar}}

Wepet-em-asech-ati |NAME3-TRANSKRIPTION = ''Wpt-m-3sḫ-jtj'' |NAME3-ERKLÄRUNG = ''Beginning of the {{ill|barley|de|Gerste|12=draft}} harvest'' |IMAGE1 = Illustration Hordeum vulgare0.jpg |IMAGE1-WIDTH = 210px }} '''Renutet''' ("The Nourishing One") denoted in the {{ill|Ancient Egyptian calendar|de|Ägyptischer Kalender|12=draft}} the beginning of the harvest season and the first month of the {{ill|Season of the Harvest|lt=Shemu|de|Schemu|12=draft}} period as the "nourishing month." From the {{ill|Predynastic Egypt|de|Prädynastik (Ägypten)|12=draft}} until the end of the {{ill|Middle Kingdom of Egypt|de|Mittleres Reich|12=draft}}, Renutet, originally the ninth month of the {{ill|Sothic calendar|1=Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar|de|Mondkalender (Altes Ägypten)|1=Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar#Der ursprüngliche Mondkalender|12=draft}}, corresponded to the period from early {{ill|February|de|Februar|12=draft}} to early {{ill|March|de|März|12=draft}}. == History == {{ill|Alan Gardiner|de|Alan Gardiner|12=draft}} and {{ill|Richard Anthony Parker|de|Richard Anthony Parker|12=draft}} theorized that Renutet underwent a calendrical shift over time, eventually moving to the eighth month by the {{ill|New Kingdom of Egypt|de|Neues Reich|12=draft}}. In the {{ill|Ebers calendar|de|Ebers-Kalender|12=draft}}, dated to around '''1517 BCE''', Renutet corresponded to the third {{ill|Peret (Ancient Egypt)|de|Peret (Altes Ägypten)|12=draft}} month, spanning from '''February 14 to March 15''' ({{ill|Elephantine|de|Elephantine|12=draft}}) and '''February 19 to March 20''' ({{ill|Memphis (Egypt)|de|Memphis (Ägypten)|12=draft}}). == Significance == Renutet, identified with the goddess {{ill|Renenutet|de|Renenutet|12=draft}}, was revered as: * '''"Lady of the Granary"''' * '''"Lady of the Fertile Land"''' * '''"Lady of the Harvest Blessing"''' During the harvest, the Egyptians made offerings to her. '''Stelae found in granaries and field boundaries''' provide evidence of this cult. The '''{{ill|Renenutet Festival|de|Renenutet-Fest|12=draft}}''' was later celebrated on '''{{ill|Pa-en-Renenutet|lt=Pharmouthi|de|Pharmouthi|12=draft}} 27''', known as the '''"Day of Measuring the {{ill|barley|de|Gerste|12=draft}}."''' In the '''Theban calendar''', this corresponded to '''{{ill|Pa-en-Chonsu|de|Pa-en-Chonsu|12=draft}} 1 (Pachon 1).''' Additionally, as a '''fertility goddess''', Renenutet was credited with ensuring the '''"emergence of flax."''' Because flax was processed into linen, she also bore the titles '''"Lady of the Garments"''' and '''"Overseer of the Temple's Clothing Chamber,"''' referring to the belief that gods were clothed in '''"the garment of Renenutet."''' Records from the '''{{ill|Solar Temple of Niuserre|de|Sonnenheiligtum des Niuserre|12=draft}}''' indicate that the '''barley harvest''' took place in '''February and March.'''{{ill|Elmar Edel|de|Elmar Edel|12=draft}}: ''Zu den Inschriften auf den Jahreszeitenreliefs der „Weltkammer“ aus dem Sonnenheiligtum des Niuserre, Teil 2.'' In: ''Nachrichten der Akademie der Wissenschaften in Göttingen.'' No. 5. Vandenhoeck & Ruprecht, Göttingen 1964, p. 200. == Name Change == The '''first Shemu month''' was later renamed '''{{ill|Pa-en-Chonsu|de|Pa-en-Chonsu|12=draft}} (Pachon).''' == See Also == * {{ill|Tebtunis lunar calendar|de|Tebtunis-Mondkalender|12=draft}} == References == * {{ill|Rolf Krauss|de|Rolf Krauss|12=draft}}: ''Sothis- und Monddaten: Studien zur astronomischen und technischen Chronologie Altägyptens.'' Gerstenberg, Hildesheim 1985, {{Falsche ISBN|3-8067-8086-X}}. * {{ill|Richard Anthony Parker|de|Richard Anthony Parker|12=draft}}: ''The calendars of ancient Egypt.'' Chicago Press, Chicago 1950. * {{ill|Siegfried Schott|de|Siegfried Schott|12=draft}}: ''Altägyptische Festdaten.'' Verlag der Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur, Mainz/ Wiesbaden 1950. == Notes == {{Navigationsleiste Ägyptische Kalendermonate bis zum Anfang des Neuen Reiches}} {{Ancient Egyptian Lunar Months}} {{draft categories| }} {{Egyptian Calendar Navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Month of the Ancient Egyptian calendar]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian lunar months]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian festivals]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Harvest festivals]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Renutet]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Lunar Months]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:pages tagged as Automatic wikipedia redirects in namespace ]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:return the see also]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] }} [[sn:Renutet]] [[de:Renutet]] [[ru:%D0%A0%D0%B5%D0%BD%D1%83%D1%82%D0%B5%D1%82]]

Wepet-em-asech-ati |NAME3-TRANSKRIPTION = ''Wpt-m-3sḫ-jtj'' |NAME3-ERKLÄRUNG = ''Beginning of the {{ill|barley|de|Gerste|12=draft|12=draft}} harvest'' |IMAGE1 = Illustration Hordeum vulgare0.jpg |IMAGE1-WIDTH = 210px }} '''Renutet''' ("The Nourishing One") denoted in the {{ill|Ancient Egyptian calendar|de|Ägyptischer Kalender|12=draft|12=draft}} the beginning of the harvest season and the first month of the {{ill|Season of the Harvest|lt=Shemu|de|Schemu|12=draft|12=draft}} period as the "nourishing month." From the {{ill|Predynastic Egypt|de|Prädynastik (Ägypten)|12=draft|12=draft}} until the end of the {{ill|Middle Kingdom of Egypt|de|Mittleres Reich|12=draft|12=draft}}, Renutet, originally the ninth month of the {{ill|Sothic calendar|1=Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar|de|Mondkalender (Altes Ägypten)|1=Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar#Der ursprüngliche Mondkalender|12=draft|12=draft}}, corresponded to the period from early {{ill|February|de|Februar|12=draft|12=draft}} to early {{ill|March|de|März|12=draft|12=draft}}. == History == {{ill|Alan Gardiner|de|Alan Gardiner|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Richard Anthony Parker|de|Richard Anthony Parker|12=draft|12=draft}} theorized that Renutet underwent a calendrical shift over time, eventually moving to the eighth month by the {{ill|New Kingdom of Egypt|de|Neues Reich|12=draft|12=draft}}. In the {{ill|Ebers calendar|de|Ebers-Kalender|12=draft|12=draft}}, dated to around '''1517 BCE''', Renutet corresponded to the third {{ill|Peret (Ancient Egypt)|de|Peret (Altes Ägypten)|12=draft|12=draft}} month, spanning from '''February 14 to March 15''' ({{ill|Elephantine|de|Elephantine|12=draft|12=draft}}) and '''February 19 to March 20''' ({{ill|Memphis (Egypt)|de|Memphis (Ägypten)|12=draft|12=draft}}). == Significance == Renutet, identified with the goddess {{ill|Renenutet|de|Renenutet|12=draft|12=draft}}, was revered as: * '''"Lady of the Granary"''' * '''"Lady of the Fertile Land"''' * '''"Lady of the Harvest Blessing"''' During the harvest, the Egyptians made offerings to her. '''Stelae found in granaries and field boundaries''' provide evidence of this cult. The '''{{ill|Renenutet Festival|de|Renenutet-Fest|12=draft|12=draft}}''' was later celebrated on '''{{ill|Pa-en-Renenutet|lt=Pharmouthi|de|Pharmouthi|12=draft|12=draft}} 27''', known as the '''"Day of Measuring the {{ill|barley|de|Gerste|12=draft|12=draft}}."''' In the '''Theban calendar''', this corresponded to '''{{ill|Pa-en-Chonsu|de|Pa-en-Chonsu|12=draft|12=draft}} 1 (Pachon 1).''' Additionally, as a '''fertility goddess''', Renenutet was credited with ensuring the '''"emergence of flax."''' Because flax was processed into linen, she also bore the titles '''"Lady of the Garments"''' and '''"Overseer of the Temple's Clothing Chamber,"''' referring to the belief that gods were clothed in '''"the garment of Renenutet."''' Records from the '''{{ill|Solar Temple of Niuserre|de|Sonnenheiligtum des Niuserre|12=draft|12=draft}}''' indicate that the '''barley harvest''' took place in '''February and March.'''{{ill|Elmar Edel|de|Elmar Edel|12=draft|12=draft}}: ''Zu den Inschriften auf den Jahreszeitenreliefs der „Weltkammer“ aus dem Sonnenheiligtum des Niuserre, Teil 2.'' In: ''Nachrichten der Akademie der Wissenschaften in Göttingen.'' No. 5. Vandenhoeck & Ruprecht, Göttingen 1964, p. 200. == Name Change == The '''first Shemu month''' was later renamed '''{{ill|Pa-en-Chonsu|de|Pa-en-Chonsu|12=draft|12=draft}} (Pachon).''' == See Also == * {{ill|Tebtunis lunar calendar|de|Tebtunis-Mondkalender|12=draft|12=draft}} == References == * {{ill|Rolf Krauss|de|Rolf Krauss|12=draft|12=draft}}: ''Sothis- und Monddaten: Studien zur astronomischen und technischen Chronologie Altägyptens.'' Records from the '''{{ill|Solar Temple of Niuserre|de|Sonnenheiligtum des Niuserre|12=draft|12=draft}}''' indicate that the '''barley harvest''' took place in '''February and March.'''{{ill|Elmar Edel|de|Elmar Edel|12=draft|12=draft}}: ''Zu den Inschriften auf den Jahreszeitenreliefs der „Weltkammer“ aus dem Sonnenheiligtum des Niuserre, Teil 2.'' In: ''Nachrichten der Akademie der Wissenschaften in Göttingen.'' No. 5. Vandenhoeck & Ruprecht, Göttingen 1964, p. 200. == Name Change == The '''first Shemu month''' was later renamed '''{{ill|Pa-en-Chonsu|de|Pa-en-Chonsu|12=draft|12=draft}} (Pachon).''' == See Also == * {{ill|Tebtunis lunar calendar|de|Tebtunis-Mondkalender|12=draft|12=draft}} == References == * {{ill|Rolf Krauss|de|Rolf Krauss|12=draft|12=draft}}: ''Sothis- und Monddaten: Studien zur astronomischen und technischen Chronologie Altägyptens.'' Gerstenberg, Hildesheim 1985, {{Falsche ISBN|3-8067-8086-X}}. * {{ill|Richard Anthony Parker|de|Richard Anthony Parker|12=draft|12=draft}}: ''The calendars of ancient Egypt.'' Chicago Press, Chicago 1950. * {{ill|Siegfried Schott|de|Siegfried Schott|12=draft|12=draft}}: ''Altägyptische Festdaten.'' Verlag der Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur, Mainz/ Wiesbaden 1950. == Notes == {{Navigationsleiste Ägyptische Kalendermonate bis zum Anfang des Neuen Reiches}} {{Ancient Egyptian Lunar Months}} {{Egyptian Calendar Navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Month of the Ancient Egyptian calendar]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian lunar months]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian festivals]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Harvest festivals]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Renutet]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Lunar Months]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:pages tagged as Automatic wikipedia redirects in namespace ]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:return the see also]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] }} [[sn:Renutet]] [[de:Renutet]] [[ru:%D0%A0%D0%B5%D0%BD%D1%83%D1%82%D0%B5%D1%82]]

revision.text:
{{Infobox Hieroglyphen |NAME = r:n-Z7-t:t |NAME-TRANSKRIPTION = |NAME-ERKLÄRUNG = {{ill|Renenutet|de|Renenutet|12=draft|12=draft}}
''Rnwtt''
''The Nourishing One'' |NAME2 = N11:Z1 S-N35B
1-nu-schemu |NAME2-TRANSKRIPTION = ''1-nw-šmw'' |NAME2-ERKLÄRUNG = ''First month of the {{ill|Season of the Harvest|lt=Shemu|de|Schemu|12=draft|12=draft}} period'' |NAME3 = F13:X1-G17-G1-O34:Aa1-M17-M17:X1-M33
Wepet-em-asech-ati |NAME3-TRANSKRIPTION = ''Wpt-m-3sḫ-jtj'' |NAME3-ERKLÄRUNG = ''Beginning of the {{ill|barley|de|Gerste|12=draft|12=draft}} harvest'' |IMAGE1 = Illustration Hordeum vulgare0.jpg |IMAGE1-WIDTH = 210px }} '''Renutet''' ("The Nourishing One") denoted in the {{ill|Ancient Egyptian calendar|de|Ägyptischer Kalender|12=draft|12=draft}} the beginning of the harvest season and the first month of the {{ill|Season of the Harvest|lt=Shemu|de|Schemu|12=draft|12=draft}} period as the "nourishing month." From the {{ill|Predynastic Egypt|de|Prädynastik (Ägypten)|12=draft|12=draft}} until the end of the {{ill|Middle Kingdom of Egypt|de|Mittleres Reich|12=draft|12=draft}}, Renutet, originally the ninth month of the {{ill|Sothic calendar|1=Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar|de|Mondkalender (Altes Ägypten)|1=Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar#Der ursprüngliche Mondkalender|12=draft|12=draft}}, corresponded to the period from early {{ill|February|de|Februar|12=draft|12=draft}} to early {{ill|March|de|März|12=draft|12=draft}}. == History == {{ill|Alan Gardiner|de|Alan Gardiner|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Richard Anthony Parker|de|Richard Anthony Parker|12=draft|12=draft}} theorized that Renutet underwent a calendrical shift over time, eventually moving to the eighth month by the {{ill|New Kingdom of Egypt|de|Neues Reich|12=draft|12=draft}}. In the {{ill|Ebers calendar|de|Ebers-Kalender|12=draft|12=draft}}, dated to around '''1517 BCE''', Renutet corresponded to the third {{ill|Peret (Ancient Egypt)|de|Peret (Altes Ägypten)|12=draft|12=draft}} month, spanning from '''February 14 to March 15''' ({{ill|Elephantine|de|Elephantine|12=draft|12=draft}}) and '''February 19 to March 20''' ({{ill|Memphis (Egypt)|de|Memphis (Ägypten)|12=draft|12=draft}}). == Significance == Renutet, identified with the goddess {{ill|Renenutet|de|Renenutet|12=draft|12=draft}}, was revered as: * '''"Lady of the Granary"''' * '''"Lady of the Fertile Land"''' * '''"Lady of the Harvest Blessing"''' During the harvest, the Egyptians made offerings to her. '''Stelae found in granaries and field boundaries''' provide evidence of this cult. The '''{{ill|Renenutet Festival|de|Renenutet-Fest|12=draft|12=draft}}''' was later celebrated on '''{{ill|Pa-en-Renenutet|lt=Pharmouthi|de|Pharmouthi|12=draft|12=draft}} 27''', known as the '''"Day of Measuring the {{ill|barley|de|Gerste|12=draft|12=draft}}."''' In the '''Theban calendar''', this corresponded to '''{{ill|Pa-en-Chonsu|de|Pa-en-Chonsu|12=draft|12=draft}} 1 (Pachon 1).''' Additionally, as a '''fertility goddess''', Renenutet was credited with ensuring the '''"emergence of flax."''' Because flax was processed into linen, she also bore the titles '''"Lady of the Garments"''' and '''"Overseer of the Temple's Clothing Chamber,"''' referring to the belief that gods were clothed in '''"the garment of Renenutet."''' Records from the '''{{ill|Solar Temple of Niuserre|de|Sonnenheiligtum des Niuserre|12=draft|12=draft}}''' indicate that the '''barley harvest''' took place in '''February and March.'''{{ill|Elmar Edel|de|Elmar Edel|12=draft|12=draft}}: ''Zu den Inschriften auf den Jahreszeitenreliefs der „Weltkammer“ aus dem Sonnenheiligtum des Niuserre, Teil 2.'' In: ''Nachrichten der Akademie der Wissenschaften in Göttingen.'' No. 5. Vandenhoeck & Ruprecht, Göttingen 1964, p. 200. == Name Change == The '''first Shemu month''' was later renamed '''{{ill|Pa-en-Chonsu|de|Pa-en-Chonsu|12=draft|12=draft}} (Pachon).''' == See Also == * {{ill|Tebtunis lunar calendar|de|Tebtunis-Mondkalender|12=draft|12=draft}} == References == * {{ill|Rolf Krauss|de|Rolf Krauss|12=draft|12=draft}}: ''Sothis- und Monddaten: Studien zur astronomischen und technischen Chronologie Altägyptens.'' Gerstenberg, Hildesheim 1985, {{Falsche ISBN|3-8067-8086-X}}. * {{ill|Richard Anthony Parker|de|Richard Anthony Parker|12=draft|12=draft}}: ''The calendars of ancient Egypt.'' Chicago Press, Chicago 1950. * {{ill|Siegfried Schott|de|Siegfried Schott|12=draft|12=draft}}: ''Altägyptische Festdaten.'' Verlag der Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur, Mainz/ Wiesbaden 1950. == Notes == {{Navigationsleiste Ägyptische Kalendermonate bis zum Anfang des Neuen Reiches}} {{Ancient Egyptian Lunar Months}} {{Egyptian Calendar Navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Month of the Ancient Egyptian calendar]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian lunar months]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian festivals]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Harvest festivals]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Renutet]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Lunar Months]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:pages tagged as Automatic wikipedia redirects in namespace ]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] }} [[sn:Renutet]] [[de:Renutet]] [[ru:%D0%A0%D0%B5%D0%BD%D1%83%D1%82%D0%B5%D1%82]]

{{Infobox Hieroglyphen |NAME = r:n-Z7-t:t |NAME-TRANSKRIPTION = |NAME-ERKLÄRUNG = {{ill|Renenutet|de|Renenutet|12=draft|12=draft}}
''Rnwtt''
''The Nourishing One'' |NAME2 = N11:Z1 S-N35B
1-nu-schemu |NAME2-TRANSKRIPTION = ''1-nw-šmw'' |NAME2-ERKLÄRUNG = ''First month of the {{ill|Season of the Harvest|lt=Shemu|de|Schemu|12=draft|12=draft}} period'' |NAME3 = F13:X1-G17-G1-O34:Aa1-M17-M17:X1-M33
Wepet-em-asech-ati |NAME3-TRANSKRIPTION = ''Wpt-m-3sḫ-jtj'' |NAME3-ERKLÄRUNG = ''Beginning of the {{ill|barley|de|Gerste|12=draft|12=draft}} harvest'' |IMAGE1 = Illustration Hordeum vulgare0.jpg |IMAGE1-WIDTH = 210px }} '''Renutet''' ("The Nourishing One") denoted in the {{ill|Ancient Egyptian calendar|de|Ägyptischer Kalender|12=draft|12=draft}} the beginning of the harvest season and the first month of the {{ill|Season of the Harvest|lt=Shemu|de|Schemu|12=draft|12=draft}} period as the "nourishing month." From the {{ill|Predynastic Egypt|de|Prädynastik (Ägypten)|12=draft|12=draft}} until the end of the {{ill|Middle Kingdom of Egypt|de|Mittleres Reich|12=draft|12=draft}}, Renutet, originally the ninth month of the {{ill|Sothic calendar|1=Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar|de|Mondkalender (Altes Ägypten)|1=Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar#Der ursprüngliche Mondkalender|12=draft|12=draft}}, corresponded to the period from early {{ill|February|de|Februar|12=draft|12=draft}} to early {{ill|March|de|März|12=draft|12=draft}}. == History == {{ill|Alan Gardiner|de|Alan Gardiner|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Richard Anthony Parker|de|Richard Anthony Parker|12=draft|12=draft}} theorized that Renutet underwent a calendrical shift over time, eventually moving to the eighth month by the {{ill|New Kingdom of Egypt|de|Neues Reich|12=draft|12=draft}}. In the {{ill|Ebers calendar|de|Ebers-Kalender|12=draft|12=draft}}, dated to around '''1517 BCE''', Renutet corresponded to the third {{ill|Peret (Ancient Egypt)|de|Peret (Altes Ägypten)|12=draft|12=draft}} month, spanning from '''February 14 to March 15''' ({{ill|Elephantine|de|Elephantine|12=draft|12=draft}}) and '''February 19 to March 20''' ({{ill|Memphis (Egypt)|de|Memphis (Ägypten)|12=draft|12=draft}}). == Significance == Renutet, identified with the goddess {{ill|Renenutet|de|Renenutet|12=draft|12=draft}}, was revered as: * '''"Lady of the Granary"''' * '''"Lady of the Fertile Land"''' * '''"Lady of the Harvest Blessing"''' During the harvest, the Egyptians made offerings to her. '''Stelae found in granaries and field boundaries''' provide evidence of this cult. The '''{{ill|Renenutet Festival|de|Renenutet-Fest|12=draft|12=draft}}''' was later celebrated on '''{{ill|Pa-en-Renenutet|lt=Pharmouthi|de|Pharmouthi|12=draft|12=draft}} 27''', known as the '''"Day of Measuring the {{ill|barley|de|Gerste|12=draft|12=draft}}."''' In the '''Theban calendar''', this corresponded to '''{{ill|Pa-en-Chonsu|de|Pa-en-Chonsu|12=draft|12=draft}} 1 (Pachon 1).''' Additionally, as a '''fertility goddess''', Renenutet was credited with ensuring the '''"emergence of flax."''' Because flax was processed into linen, she also bore the titles '''"Lady of the Garments"''' and '''"Overseer of the Temple's Clothing Chamber,"''' referring to the belief that gods were clothed in '''"the garment of Renenutet."''' Records from the '''{{ill|Solar Temple of Niuserre|de|Sonnenheiligtum des Niuserre|12=draft|12=draft}}''' indicate that the '''barley harvest''' took place in '''February and March.'''{{ill|Elmar Edel|de|Elmar Edel|12=draft|12=draft}}: ''Zu den Inschriften auf den Jahreszeitenreliefs der „Weltkammer“ aus dem Sonnenheiligtum des Niuserre, Teil 2.'' In: ''Nachrichten der Akademie der Wissenschaften in Göttingen.'' No. 5. Vandenhoeck & Ruprecht, Göttingen 1964, p. 200. == Name Change == The '''first Shemu month''' was later renamed '''{{ill|Pa-en-Chonsu|de|Pa-en-Chonsu|12=draft|12=draft}} (Pachon).''' == See Also == * {{ill|Tebtunis lunar calendar|de|Tebtunis-Mondkalender|12=draft|12=draft}} == References == * {{ill|Rolf Krauss|de|Rolf Krauss|12=draft|12=draft}}: ''Sothis- und Monddaten: Studien zur astronomischen und technischen Chronologie Altägyptens.'' Gerstenberg, Hildesheim 1985, {{Falsche ISBN|3-8067-8086-X}}. * {{ill|Richard Anthony Parker|de|Richard Anthony Parker|12=draft|12=draft}}: ''The calendars of ancient Egypt.'' Chicago Press, Chicago 1950. * {{ill|Siegfried Schott|de|Siegfried Schott|12=draft|12=draft}}: ''Altägyptische Festdaten.'' Verlag der Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur, Mainz/ Wiesbaden 1950. == Notes == {{Navigationsleiste Ägyptische Kalendermonate bis zum Anfang des Neuen Reiches}} {{Ancient Egyptian Lunar Months}} {{Egyptian Calendar Navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Month of the Ancient Egyptian calendar]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian lunar months]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian festivals]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Harvest festivals]] [[Category:Renutet]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Lunar Months]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:pages tagged as Automatic wikipedia redirects in namespace ]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Renutet]] [[de:Renutet]] [[ru:%D0%A0%D0%B5%D0%BD%D1%83%D1%82%D0%B5%D1%82]]

{{Infobox Hieroglyphen |NAME = r:n-Z7-t:t |NAME-TRANSKRIPTION = |NAME-ERKLÄRUNG = {{ill|Renenutet|de|Renenutet|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}

''The Nourishing One'' |NAME2 = N11:Z1 S-N35B
1-nu-schemu |NAME2-TRANSKRIPTION = ''1-nw-šmw'' |NAME2-ERKLÄRUNG = ''First month of the {{ill|Season of the Harvest|lt=Shemu|de|Schemu|1=Season of the Harvest|lt=Schemu|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} period'' |NAME3 = F13:X1-G17-G1-O34:Aa1-M17-M17:X1-M33
Wepet-em-asech-ati |NAME3-TRANSKRIPTION = ''Wpt-m-3sḫ-jtj'' |NAME3-ERKLÄRUNG = ''Beginning of the {{ill|barley|de|Gerste|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} harvest'' |IMAGE1 = Illustration Hordeum vulgare0.jpg |IMAGE1-WIDTH = 210px }} '''Renutet''' ("The Nourishing One") denoted in the {{ill|Ancient Egyptian calendar|de|Ägyptischer Kalender|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} the beginning of the harvest season and the first month of the {{ill|Season of the Harvest|lt=Shemu|de|Schemu|1=Season of the Harvest|lt=Schemu|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} period as the "nourishing month." From the {{ill|Predynastic Egypt|de|Prädynastik (Ägypten)|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} until the end of the {{ill|Middle Kingdom of Egypt|de|Mittleres Reich|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}, Renutet, originally the ninth month of the {{ill|Sothic calendar|1=Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar|de|Mondkalender (Altes Ägypten)|1=Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar#Der ursprüngliche Mondkalender|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}, corresponded to the period from early {{ill|February|de|Februar|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} to early {{ill|March|de|März|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}. == History == {{ill|Alan Gardiner|de|Alan Gardiner|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Richard Anthony Parker|de|Richard Anthony Parker|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} theorized that Renutet underwent a calendrical shift over time, eventually moving to the eighth month by the {{ill|New Kingdom of Egypt|de|Neues Reich|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}. In the {{ill|Ebers calendar|de|Ebers-Kalender|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}, dated to around '''1517 BCE''', Renutet corresponded to the third {{ill|Peret (Ancient Egypt)|de|Peret (Altes Ägypten)|1=Season of the Emergence|lt=Peret|2=de|3=Peret (Altes Ägypten)|4=ja|5=ペレト|6=zh|7=播種季|8=ru|9=Перет|10=uk|11=Перет|12=draft|13=Season of the Emergence|14=es|15=Estación de la emergencia|16=fr|17=Peret|18=pl|19=Peret|20=ro|21=Peret|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} month, spanning from '''February 14 to March 15''' ({{ill|Elephantine|de|Elephantine|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}) and '''February 19 to March 20''' ({{ill|Memphis (Egypt)|de|Memphis (Ägypten)|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}). == Significance == Renutet, identified with the goddess {{ill|Renenutet|de|Renenutet|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}, was revered as: * '''"Lady of the Granary"''' * '''"Lady of the Fertile Land"''' * '''"Lady of the Harvest Blessing"''' During the harvest, the Egyptians made offerings to her. '''Stelae found in granaries and field boundaries''' provide evidence of this cult. The '''{{ill|Renenutet Festival|de|Renenutet-Fest|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}''' was later celebrated on '''{{ill|Pa-en-Renenutet|lt=Pharmouthi|de|Pharmouthi|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} 27''', known as the '''"Day of Measuring the {{ill|barley|de|Gerste|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}."''' In the '''Theban calendar''', this corresponded to '''{{ill|Pa-en-Chonsu|de|Pa-en-Chonsu|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} 1 (Pachon 1).''' Additionally, as a '''fertility goddess''', Renenutet was credited with ensuring the '''"emergence of flax."''' Because flax was processed into linen, she also bore the titles '''"Lady of the Garments"''' and '''"Overseer of the Temple's Clothing Chamber,"''' referring to the belief that gods were clothed in '''"the garment of Renenutet."''' Records from the '''{{ill|Solar Temple of Niuserre|de|Sonnenheiligtum des Niuserre|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}''' indicate that the '''barley harvest''' took place in '''February and March.'''{{ill|Elmar Edel|de|Elmar Edel|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}: ''Zu den Inschriften auf den Jahreszeitenreliefs der „Weltkammer“ aus dem Sonnenheiligtum des Niuserre, Teil 2.'' In: ''Nachrichten der Akademie der Wissenschaften in Göttingen.'' No. 5. Vandenhoeck & Ruprecht, Göttingen 1964, p. 200. == Name Change == The '''first Shemu month''' was later renamed '''{{ill|Pa-en-Chonsu|de|Pa-en-Chonsu|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} (Pachon).''' == See Also == * {{ill|Tebtunis lunar calendar|de|Tebtunis-Mondkalender|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} == References == * {{ill|Rolf Krauss|de|Rolf Krauss|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}: ''Sothis- und Monddaten: Studien zur astronomischen und technischen Chronologie Altägyptens.'' Gerstenberg, Hildesheim 1985, {{Falsche ISBN|3-8067-8086-X}}. * {{ill|Richard Anthony Parker|de|Richard Anthony Parker|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}: ''The calendars of ancient Egypt.'' Chicago Press, Chicago 1950. * {{ill|Siegfried Schott|de|Siegfried Schott|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}: ''Altägyptische Festdaten.'' Verlag der Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur, Mainz/ Wiesbaden 1950. == Notes == {{Navigationsleiste Ägyptische Kalendermonate bis zum Anfang des Neuen Reiches}} {{Ancient Egyptian Lunar Months}} {{Egyptian Calendar Navbar}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Month of the Ancient Egyptian calendar]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian lunar months]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian festivals]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Harvest festivals]] [[Category:Renutet]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Lunar Months]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Renutet]] [[de:Renutet]] [[ru:%D0%A0%D0%B5%D0%BD%D1%83%D1%82%D0%B5%D1%82]]

''Rnwtt''
''The Nourishing One'' |NAME2 = N11:Z1 S-N35B
1-nu-schemu |NAME2-TRANSKRIPTION = ''1-nw-šmw'' |NAME2-ERKLÄRUNG = ''First month of the {{ill|Season of the Harvest|lt=Shemu|de|Schemu|1=Season of the Harvest|lt=Schemu|qq=draft}} period'' |NAME3 = F13:X1-G17-G1-O34:Aa1-M17-M17:X1-M33
Wepet-em-asech-ati |NAME3-TRANSKRIPTION = ''Wpt-m-3sḫ-jtj'' |NAME3-ERKLÄRUNG = ''Beginning of the {{ill|barley|de|Gerste|qq=draft}} harvest'' |IMAGE1 = Illustration Hordeum vulgare0.jpg |IMAGE1-WIDTH = 210px }} '''Renutet''' ("The Nourishing One") denoted in the {{ill|Ancient Egyptian calendar|de|Ägyptischer Kalender|qq=draft}} the beginning of the harvest season and the first month of the {{ill|Season of the Harvest|lt=Shemu|de|Schemu|1=Season of the Harvest|lt=Schemu|qq=draft}} period as the "nourishing month." From the {{ill|Predynastic Egypt|de|Prädynastik (Ägypten)|qq=draft}} until the end of the {{ill|Middle Kingdom of Egypt|de|Mittleres Reich|qq=draft}}, Renutet, originally the ninth month of the {{ill|Sothic calendar|1=Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar|de|Mondkalender (Altes Ägypten)|1=Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar#Der ursprüngliche Mondkalender|qq=draft}}, corresponded to the period from early {{ill|February|de|Februar|qq=draft}} to early {{ill|March|de|März|qq=draft}}. == History == {{ill|Alan Gardiner|de|Alan Gardiner|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Richard Anthony Parker|de|Richard Anthony Parker|qq=draft}} theorized that Renutet underwent a calendrical shift over time, eventually moving to the eighth month by the {{ill|New Kingdom of Egypt|de|Neues Reich|qq=draft}}. In the {{ill|Ebers calendar|de|Ebers-Kalender|qq=draft}}, dated to around '''1517 BCE''', Renutet corresponded to the third {{ill|Peret (Ancient Egypt)|de|Peret (Altes Ägypten)|1=Season of the Emergence|lt=Peret|2=de|3=Peret (Altes Ägypten)|4=ja|5=ペレト|6=zh|7=播種季|8=ru|9=Перет|10=uk|11=Перет|qq=draft|13=Season of the Emergence|14=es|15=Estación de la emergencia|16=fr|17=Peret|18=pl|19=Peret|20=ro|21=Peret|qq=draft}} month, spanning from '''February 14 to March 15''' ({{ill|Elephantine|de|Elephantine|qq=draft}}) and '''February 19 to March 20''' ({{ill|Memphis (Egypt)|de|Memphis (Ägypten)|qq=draft}}). == Significance == Renutet, identified with the goddess {{ill|Renenutet|de|Renenutet|qq=draft}}, was revered as: * '''"Lady of the Granary"''' * '''"Lady of the Fertile Land"''' * '''"Lady of the Harvest Blessing"''' During the harvest, the Egyptians made offerings to her. '''Stelae found in granaries and field boundaries''' provide evidence of this cult. The '''{{ill|Renenutet Festival|de|Renenutet-Fest|qq=draft}}''' was later celebrated on '''{{ill|Pa-en-Renenutet|lt=Pharmouthi|de|Pharmouthi|qq=draft}} 27''', known as the '''"Day of Measuring the {{ill|barley|de|Gerste|qq=draft}}."''' In the '''Theban calendar''', this corresponded to '''{{ill|Pa-en-Chonsu|1=Pa-en-Chonsu|de|Pa-en-Chonsu|1=Pa-en-Chonsu|qq=draft}} 1 (Pachon 1).''' Additionally, as a '''fertility goddess''', Renenutet was credited with ensuring the '''"emergence of flax."''' Because flax was processed into linen, she also bore the titles '''"Lady of the Garments"''' and '''"Overseer of the Temple's Clothing Chamber,"''' referring to the belief that gods were clothed in '''"the garment of Renenutet."''' Records from the '''{{ill|Solar Temple of Niuserre|de|Sonnenheiligtum des Niuserre|qq=draft}}''' indicate that the '''barley harvest''' took place in '''February and March.'''{{ill|Elmar Edel|de|Elmar Edel|qq=draft}}: ''Zu den Inschriften auf den Jahreszeitenreliefs der „Weltkammer“ aus dem Sonnenheiligtum des Niuserre, Teil 2.'' In: ''Nachrichten der Akademie der Wissenschaften in Göttingen.'' No. 5. Vandenhoeck & Ruprecht, Göttingen 1964, p. 200. == Name Change == The '''first Shemu month''' was later renamed '''{{ill|Pa-en-Chonsu|1=Pa-en-Chonsu|de|Pa-en-Chonsu|1=Pa-en-Chonsu|qq=draft}} (Pachon).''' == See Also == * {{ill|Tebtunis lunar calendar|de|Tebtunis-Mondkalender|qq=draft}} == References == * {{ill|Rolf Krauss|de|Rolf Krauss|qq=draft}}: ''Sothis- und Monddaten: Studien zur astronomischen und technischen Chronologie Altägyptens.'' Gerstenberg, Hildesheim 1985, {{Falsche ISBN|3-8067-8086-X}}. * {{ill|Richard Anthony Parker|de|Richard Anthony Parker|qq=draft}}: ''The calendars of ancient Egypt.'' Chicago Press, Chicago 1950. * {{ill|Siegfried Schott|de|Siegfried Schott|qq=draft}}: ''Altägyptische Festdaten.'' Verlag der Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur, Mainz/ Wiesbaden 1950. == Notes == {{Navigationsleiste Ägyptische Kalendermonate bis zum Anfang des Neuen Reiches}} {{Ancient Egyptian Lunar Months}} {{Egyptian Calendar Navbar}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Month of the Ancient Egyptian calendar]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian lunar months]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian festivals]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Harvest festivals]] [[Category:Renutet]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Lunar Months]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Renutet]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Renutet]] [[de:Renutet]] [[ru:%D0%A0%D0%B5%D0%BD%D1%83%D1%82%D0%B5%D1%82]]

1-nu-schemu |NAME2-TRANSKRIPTION = ''1-nw-šmw'' |NAME2-ERKLÄRUNG = ''First month of the {{ill|Season of the Harvest|lt=Shemu|de|Schemu|1=Season of the Harvest|lt=Schemu|qq=draft}} period'' |NAME3 = F13:X1-G17-G1-O34:Aa1-M17-M17:X1-M33
Wepet-em-asech-ati |NAME3-TRANSKRIPTION = ''Wpt-m-3sḫ-jtj'' |NAME3-ERKLÄRUNG = ''Beginning of the {{ill|barley|de|Gerste|qq=draft}} harvest'' |IMAGE1 = Illustration Hordeum vulgare0.jpg |IMAGE1-WIDTH = 210px }} '''Renutet''' ("The Nourishing One") denoted in the {{ill|Ancient Egyptian calendar|de|Ägyptischer Kalender|qq=draft}} the beginning of the harvest season and the first month of the {{ill|Season of the Harvest|lt=Shemu|de|Schemu|1=Season of the Harvest|lt=Schemu|qq=draft}} period as the "nourishing month." From the {{ill|Predynastic Egypt|de|Prädynastik (Ägypten)|qq=draft}} until the end of the {{ill|Middle Kingdom of Egypt|de|Mittleres Reich|qq=draft}}, Renutet, originally the ninth month of the {{ill|Sothic calendar|1=Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar|de|Mondkalender (Altes Ägypten)|1=Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar#Der ursprüngliche Mondkalender|qq=draft}}, corresponded to the period from early {{ill|February|de|Februar|qq=draft}} to early {{ill|March|de|März|qq=draft}}. == History == {{ill|Alan Gardiner|de|Alan Gardiner|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Richard Anthony Parker|de|Richard Anthony Parker|qq=draft}} theorized that Renutet underwent a calendrical shift over time, eventually moving to the eighth month by the {{ill|New Kingdom of Egypt|de|Neues Reich|qq=draft}}. In the {{ill|Ebers calendar|de|Ebers-Kalender|qq=draft}}, dated to around '''1517 BCE''', Renutet corresponded to the third {{ill|Peret (Ancient Egypt)|de|Peret (Altes Ägypten)|1=Season of the Emergence|lt=Peret|2=de|3=Peret (Altes Ägypten)|4=ja|5=ペレト|6=zh|7=播種季|8=ru|9=Перет|10=uk|11=Перет|qq=draft|13=Season of the Emergence|14=es|15=Estación de la emergencia|16=fr|17=Peret|18=pl|19=Peret|20=ro|21=Peret|qq=draft}} month, spanning from '''February 14 to March 15''' ({{ill|Elephantine|de|Elephantine|qq=draft}}) and '''February 19 to March 20''' ({{ill|Memphis (Egypt)|de|Memphis (Ägypten)|qq=draft}}). == Significance == Renutet, identified with the goddess {{ill|Renenutet|de|Renenutet|qq=draft}}, was revered as: * '''"Lady of the Granary"''' * '''"Lady of the Fertile Land"''' * '''"Lady of the Harvest Blessing"''' During the harvest, the Egyptians made offerings to her. '''Stelae found in granaries and field boundaries''' provide evidence of this cult. The '''{{ill|Renenutet Festival|de|Renenutet-Fest|qq=draft}}''' was later celebrated on '''{{ill|Pa-en-Renenutet|lt=Pharmouthi|de|Pharmouthi|qq=draft}} 27''', known as the '''"Day of Measuring the {{ill|barley|de|Gerste|qq=draft}}."''' In the '''Theban calendar''', this corresponded to '''{{ill|Pa-en-Chonsu|1=Pa-en-Chonsu|de|Pa-en-Chonsu|1=Pa-en-Chonsu|qq=draft}} 1 (Pachon 1).''' Additionally, as a '''fertility goddess''', Renenutet was credited with ensuring the '''"emergence of flax."''' Because flax was processed into linen, she also bore the titles '''"Lady of the Garments"''' and '''"Overseer of the Temple's Clothing Chamber,"''' referring to the belief that gods were clothed in '''"the garment of Renenutet."''' Records from the '''{{ill|Solar Temple of Niuserre|de|Sonnenheiligtum des Niuserre|qq=draft}}''' indicate that the '''barley harvest''' took place in '''February and March.'''{{ill|Elmar Edel|de|Elmar Edel|qq=draft}}: ''Zu den Inschriften auf den Jahreszeitenreliefs der „Weltkammer“ aus dem Sonnenheiligtum des Niuserre, Teil 2.'' In: ''Nachrichten der Akademie der Wissenschaften in Göttingen.'' No. 5. Vandenhoeck & Ruprecht, Göttingen 1964, p. 200. == Name Change == The '''first Shemu month''' was later renamed '''{{ill|Pa-en-Chonsu|1=Pa-en-Chonsu|de|Pa-en-Chonsu|1=Pa-en-Chonsu|qq=draft}} (Pachon).''' == See Also == * {{ill|Tebtunis lunar calendar|de|Tebtunis-Mondkalender|qq=draft}} == References == * {{ill|Rolf Krauss|de|Rolf Krauss|qq=draft}}: ''Sothis- und Monddaten: Studien zur astronomischen und technischen Chronologie Altägyptens.'' Gerstenberg, Hildesheim 1985, {{Falsche ISBN|3-8067-8086-X}}. * {{ill|Richard Anthony Parker|de|Richard Anthony Parker|qq=draft}}: ''The calendars of ancient Egypt.'' Chicago Press, Chicago 1950. * {{ill|Siegfried Schott|de|Siegfried Schott|qq=draft}}: ''Altägyptische Festdaten.'' Verlag der Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur, Mainz/ Wiesbaden 1950. == Notes == {{Navigationsleiste Ägyptische Kalendermonate bis zum Anfang des Neuen Reiches}} {{Ancient Egyptian Lunar Months}} {{Egyptian Calendar Navbar}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Month of the Ancient Egyptian calendar]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian lunar months]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian festivals]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Pages without German translation box]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Renutet]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Harvest festivals]] [[Category:Renutet]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Lunar Months]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Renutet]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Renutet]] [[de:Renutet]] [[ru:%D0%A0%D0%B5%D0%BD%D1%83%D1%82%D0%B5%D1%82]] {{translated page|de|Renutet|version=|comment=Imported full de history, revisions duplicated and version not here due to an error with my bot related to the German being under the same pagename as the english}}

'''Stelae found in granaries and field boundaries''' provide evidence of this cult. Because flax was processed into linen, she also bore the titles '''"Lady of the Garments"''' and '''"Overseer of the Temple's Clothing Chamber,"''' referring to the belief that gods were clothed in '''"the garment of Renenutet."''' ''The Nourishing One'' |NAME2 = N11:Z1 S-N35B
1-nu-schemu |NAME2-TRANSKRIPTION = ''1-nw-šmw'' |NAME2-ERKLÄRUNG = ''First month of the {{ill|Season of the Harvest|lt=Shemu|de|Schemu|1=Season of the Harvest|lt=Schemu|qq=draft|qq=draft}} period'' |NAME3 = F13:X1-G17-G1-O34:Aa1-M17-M17:X1-M33
Wepet-em-asech-ati |NAME3-TRANSKRIPTION = ''Wpt-m-3sḫ-jtj'' |NAME3-ERKLÄRUNG = ''Beginning of the {{ill|barley|de|Gerste|qq=draft|qq=draft}} harvest'' |IMAGE1 = Illustration Hordeum vulgare0.jpg |IMAGE1-WIDTH = 210px }} '''Renutet''' ("The Nourishing One") denoted in the {{ill|Ancient Egyptian calendar|de|Ägyptischer Kalender|qq=draft|qq=draft}} the beginning of the harvest season and the first month of the {{ill|Season of the Harvest|lt=Shemu|de|Schemu|1=Season of the Harvest|lt=Schemu|qq=draft|qq=draft}} period as the "nourishing month." From the {{ill|Predynastic Egypt|de|Prädynastik (Ägypten)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} until the end of the {{ill|Middle Kingdom of Egypt|de|Mittleres Reich|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Renutet, originally the ninth month of the {{ill|Sothic calendar|1=Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar|de|Mondkalender (Altes Ägypten)|1=Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar#Der ursprüngliche Mondkalender|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, corresponded to the period from early {{ill|February|de|Februar|qq=draft|qq=draft}} to early {{ill|March|de|März|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == History == {{ill|Alan Gardiner|de|Alan Gardiner|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Richard Anthony Parker|de|Richard Anthony Parker|qq=draft|qq=draft}} theorized that Renutet underwent a calendrical shift over time, eventually moving to the eighth month by the {{ill|New Kingdom of Egypt|de|Neues Reich|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. In the {{ill|Ebers calendar|de|Ebers-Kalender|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, dated to around '''1517 BCE''', Renutet corresponded to the third {{ill|Peret (Ancient Egypt)|de|Peret (Altes Ägypten)|1=Season of the Emergence|lt=Peret|2=de|3=Peret (Altes Ägypten)|4=ja|5=ペレト|6=zh|7=播種季|8=ru|9=Перет|10=uk|11=Перет|qq=draft|13=Season of the Emergence|14=es|15=Estación de la emergencia|16=fr|17=Peret|18=pl|19=Peret|20=ro|21=Peret|qq=draft|qq=draft}} month, spanning from '''February 14 to March 15''' ({{ill|Elephantine|de|Elephantine|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) and '''February 19 to March 20''' ({{ill|Memphis (Egypt)|de|Memphis (Ägypten)|qq=draft|qq=draft}}). == Significance == Renutet, identified with the goddess {{ill|Renenutet|de|Renenutet|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, was revered as: * '''"Lady of the Granary"''' * '''"Lady of the Fertile Land"''' * '''"Lady of the Harvest Blessing"''' During the harvest, the Egyptians made offerings to her. '''Stelae found in granaries and field boundaries''' provide evidence of this cult. The '''{{ill|Renenutet Festival|de|Renenutet-Fest|qq=draft|qq=draft}}''' was later celebrated on '''{{ill|Pa-en-Renenutet|lt=Pharmouthi|de|Pharmouthi|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 27''', known as the '''"Day of Measuring the {{ill|barley|de|Gerste|qq=draft|qq=draft}}."''' In the '''Theban calendar''', this corresponded to '''{{ill|Pa-en-Chonsu|1=Pa-en-Chonsu|de|Pa-en-Chonsu|1=Pa-en-Chonsu|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 1 (Pachon 1).''' Additionally, as a '''fertility goddess''', Renenutet was credited with ensuring the '''"emergence of flax."''' Because flax was processed into linen, she also bore the titles '''"Lady of the Garments"''' and '''"Overseer of the Temple's Clothing Chamber,"''' referring to the belief that gods were clothed in '''"the garment of Renenutet."''' Records from the '''{{ill|Solar Temple of Niuserre|de|Sonnenheiligtum des Niuserre|qq=draft|qq=draft}}''' indicate that the '''barley harvest''' took place in '''February and March.'''{{ill|Elmar Edel|de|Elmar Edel|qq=draft|qq=draft}}: ''Zu den Inschriften auf den Jahreszeitenreliefs der „Weltkammer“ aus dem Sonnenheiligtum des Niuserre, Teil 2.'' In: ''Nachrichten der Akademie der Wissenschaften in Göttingen.'' No. 5. Vandenhoeck & Ruprecht, Göttingen 1964, p. 200. == Name Change == The '''first Shemu month''' was later renamed '''{{ill|Pa-en-Chonsu|1=Pa-en-Chonsu|de|Pa-en-Chonsu|1=Pa-en-Chonsu|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Pachon).''' == See Also == * {{ill|Tebtunis lunar calendar|de|Tebtunis-Mondkalender|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == References == * {{ill|Rolf Krauss|de|Rolf Krauss|qq=draft|qq=draft}}: ''Sothis- und Monddaten: Studien zur astronomischen und technischen Chronologie Altägyptens.'' Gerstenberg, Hildesheim 1985, {{Falsche ISBN|3-8067-8086-X}}. * {{ill|Richard Anthony Parker|de|Richard Anthony Parker|qq=draft|qq=draft}}: ''The calendars of ancient Egypt.'' Chicago Press, Chicago 1950. * {{ill|Siegfried Schott|de|Siegfried Schott|qq=draft|qq=draft}}: ''Altägyptische Festdaten.'' Verlag der Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur, Mainz/ Wiesbaden 1950. == Notes == {{Navigationsleiste Ägyptische Kalendermonate bis zum Anfang des Neuen Reiches}} {{Ancient Egyptian Lunar Months}} {{Egyptian Calendar Navbar}} {{translated page|de|Renutet|version=|comment=Imported full de history, revisions duplicated and version not here due to an error with my bot related to the German being under the same pagename as the english}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Month of the Ancient Egyptian calendar]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian lunar months]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian festivals]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Harvest festivals]] [[Category:Renutet]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Lunar Months]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[sn:Renutet]] [[de:Renutet]] [[ru:%D0%A0%D0%B5%D0%BD%D1%83%D1%82%D0%B5%D1%82]]

{{Infobox hieroglyphs |NAME = r:n-Z7-t:t |NAME-TRANSKRIPTION = |NAME-ERKLÄRUNG = {{ill|Renenutet|de|Renenutet|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Renenutet|qq=}}
''Rnwtt''
''The Nourishing One'' |NAME2 = N11:Z1 S-N35B
1-nu-schemu |NAME2-TRANSKRIPTION = ''1-nw-šmw'' |NAME2-ERKLÄRUNG = ''First month of the {{ill|Season of the Harvest|lt=Shemu|de|Schemu|1=Season of the Harvest|lt=Schemu|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Season of the Harvest|13=User:Immanuelle/Season of the Harvest|qq=}} period'' |NAME3 = F13:X1-G17-G1-O34:Aa1-M17-M17:X1-M33
Wepet-em-asech-ati |NAME3-TRANSKRIPTION = ''Wpt-m-3sḫ-jtj'' |NAME3-ERKLÄRUNG = ''Beginning of the {{ill|barley|de|Gerste|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/barley|qq=}} harvest'' |IMAGE1 = Illustration Hordeum vulgare0.jpg |IMAGE1-WIDTH = 210px }} '''Renutet''' ("The Nourishing One") denoted in the {{ill|Ancient Egyptian calendar|de|Ägyptischer Kalender|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian calendar|qq=}} the beginning of the harvest season and the first month of the {{ill|Season of the Harvest|lt=Shemu|de|Schemu|1=Season of the Harvest|lt=Schemu|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Season of the Harvest|13=User:Immanuelle/Season of the Harvest|qq=}} period as the "nourishing month." From the {{ill|Predynastic Egypt|de|Prädynastik (Ägypten)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Predynastic Egypt|qq=}} until the end of the {{ill|Middle Kingdom of Egypt|de|Mittleres Reich|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Middle Kingdom of Egypt|qq=}}, Renutet, originally the ninth month of the {{ill|Sothic calendar|1=Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar|de|Mondkalender (Altes Ägypten)|1=Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar#Der ursprüngliche Mondkalender|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sothic calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar|qq=}}, corresponded to the period from early {{ill|February|de|Februar|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/February|qq=}} to early {{ill|March|de|März|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/March|qq=}}. == History == {{ill|Alan Gardiner|de|Alan Gardiner|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Alan Gardiner|qq=}} and {{ill|Richard Anthony Parker|de|Richard Anthony Parker|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Richard Anthony Parker|qq=}} theorized that Renutet underwent a calendrical shift over time, eventually moving to the eighth month by the {{ill|New Kingdom of Egypt|de|Neues Reich|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Kingdom of Egypt|qq=}}. In the {{ill|Ebers calendar|de|Ebers-Kalender|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebers calendar|qq=}}, dated to around '''1517 BCE''', Renutet corresponded to the third {{ill|Peret (Ancient Egypt)|de|Peret (Altes Ägypten)|1=Season of the Emergence|lt=Peret|2=de|3=Peret (Altes Ägypten)|4=ja|5=ペレト|6=zh|7=播種季|8=ru|9=Перет|10=uk|11=Перет|qq=draft|13=Season of the Emergence|14=es|15=Estación de la emergencia|16=fr|17=Peret|18=pl|19=Peret|20=ro|21=Peret|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Peret (Ancient Egypt)|13=User:Immanuelle/Season of the Emergence|qq=}} month, spanning from '''February 14 to March 15''' ({{ill|Elephantine|de|Elephantine|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Elephantine|qq=}}) and '''February 19 to March 20''' ({{ill|Memphis (Egypt)|de|Memphis (Ägypten)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Memphis (Egypt)|qq=}}). == Significance == Renutet, identified with the goddess {{ill|Renenutet|de|Renenutet|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Renenutet|qq=}}, was revered as: * '''"Lady of the Granary"''' * '''"Lady of the Fertile Land"''' * '''"Lady of the Harvest Blessing"''' During the harvest, the Egyptians made offerings to her. '''Stelae found in granaries and field boundaries''' provide evidence of this cult. The '''{{ill|Renenutet Festival|de|Renenutet-Fest|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Renenutet Festival|qq=}}''' was later celebrated on '''{{ill|Pa-en-Renenutet|lt=Pharmouthi|de|Pharmouthi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pa-en-Renenutet|qq=}} 27''', known as the '''"Day of Measuring the {{ill|barley|de|Gerste|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/barley|qq=}}."''' In the '''Theban calendar''', this corresponded to '''{{ill|Pa-en-Chonsu|1=Pa-en-Chonsu|de|Pa-en-Chonsu|1=Pa-en-Chonsu|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pa-en-Chonsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Pa-en-Chonsu|qq=}} 1 (Pachon 1).''' Additionally, as a '''fertility goddess''', Renenutet was credited with ensuring the '''"emergence of flax."''' Because flax was processed into linen, she also bore the titles '''"Lady of the Garments"''' and '''"Overseer of the Temple's Clothing Chamber,"''' referring to the belief that gods were clothed in '''"the garment of Renenutet."''' Records from the '''{{ill|Solar Temple of Niuserre|de|Sonnenheiligtum des Niuserre|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Solar Temple of Niuserre|qq=}}''' indicate that the '''barley harvest''' took place in '''February and March.'''{{ill|Elmar Edel|de|Elmar Edel|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Elmar Edel|qq=}}: ''Zu den Inschriften auf den Jahreszeitenreliefs der „Weltkammer“ aus dem Sonnenheiligtum des Niuserre, Teil 2.'' In: ''Nachrichten der Akademie der Wissenschaften in Göttingen.'' No. 5. Vandenhoeck & Ruprecht, Göttingen 1964, p. 200. == Name Change == The '''first Shemu month''' was later renamed '''{{ill|Pa-en-Chonsu|1=Pa-en-Chonsu|de|Pa-en-Chonsu|1=Pa-en-Chonsu|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pa-en-Chonsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Pa-en-Chonsu|qq=}} (Pachon).''' == See Also == * {{ill|Tebtunis lunar calendar|de|Tebtunis-Mondkalender|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tebtunis lunar calendar|qq=}} == References == * {{ill|Rolf Krauss|de|Rolf Krauss|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rolf Krauss|qq=}}: ''Sothis- und Monddaten: Studien zur astronomischen und technischen Chronologie Altägyptens.'' Gerstenberg, Hildesheim 1985, {{Falsche ISBN|3-8067-8086-X}}. * {{ill|Richard Anthony Parker|de|Richard Anthony Parker|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Richard Anthony Parker|qq=}}: ''The calendars of ancient Egypt.'' Chicago Press, Chicago 1950. * {{ill|Siegfried Schott|de|Siegfried Schott|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Siegfried Schott|qq=}}: ''Altägyptische Festdaten.'' Verlag der Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur, Mainz/ Wiesbaden 1950. == Notes == {{Navigationsleiste Ägyptische Kalendermonate bis zum Anfang des Neuen Reiches}} {{Ancient Egyptian Lunar Months}} [[Category:pages that had Egyptian Calendar Navbar]] {{translated page|de|Renutet|version=|comment=Imported full de history, revisions duplicated and version not here due to an error with my bot related to the German being under the same pagename as the english}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Month of the Ancient Egyptian calendar]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian lunar months]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian festivals]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Harvest festivals]] [[Category:Renutet]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Lunar Months]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[sn:Renutet]] [[de:Renutet]] [[ru:%D0%A0%D0%B5%D0%BD%D1%83%D1%82%D0%B5%D1%82]] {{Egyptian Calendar Navbar}}

{{Infobox hieroglyphs |NAME = r:n-Z7-t:t |NAME-TRANSKRIPTION = |NAME-ERKLÄRUNG = [[:en:Renenutet|Renenutet]]
''Rnwtt''
''The Nourishing One'' |NAME2 = N11:Z1 S-N35B
1-nu-schemu |NAME2-TRANSKRIPTION = ''1-nw-šmw'' |NAME2-ERKLÄRUNG = ''First month of the [[:en:Season of the Harvest|Schemu]] period'' |NAME3 = F13:X1-G17-G1-O34:Aa1-M17-M17:X1-M33
Wepet-em-asech-ati |NAME3-TRANSKRIPTION = ''Wpt-m-3sḫ-jtj'' |NAME3-ERKLÄRUNG = ''Beginning of the [[:en:Barley|barley]] harvest'' |IMAGE1 = Illustration Hordeum vulgare0.jpg |IMAGE1-WIDTH = 210px }} '''Renutet''' ("The Nourishing One") denoted in the [[:en:Egyptian calendar|Ancient Egyptian calendar]] the beginning of the harvest season and the first month of the [[:en:Season of the Harvest|Schemu]] period as the "nourishing month." From the {{ill|Predynastic Egypt|de|Prädynastik (Ägypten)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Predynastic Egypt|qq=}} until the end of the [[:en:Middle Kingdom of Egypt|Middle Kingdom of Egypt]], Renutet, originally the ninth month of the {{ill|Sothic calendar|1=Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar|de|Mondkalender (Altes Ägypten)|1=Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar#Der ursprüngliche Mondkalender|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sothic calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar|qq=}}, corresponded to the period from early [[:en:February|February]] to early [[:en:March|March]]. == History == [[:en:Alan Gardiner|Alan Gardiner]] and [[:en:Richard Anthony Parker|Richard Anthony Parker]] theorized that Renutet underwent a calendrical shift over time, eventually moving to the eighth month by the [[:en:New Kingdom of Egypt|New Kingdom of Egypt]]. In the {{ill|Ebers calendar|de|Ebers-Kalender|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebers calendar|qq=}}, dated to around '''1517 BCE''', Renutet corresponded to the third [[:en:Season of the Emergence|Peret]] month, spanning from '''February 14 to March 15''' ([[:en:Elephantine|Elephantine]]) and '''February 19 to March 20''' ([[:en:Memphis, Egypt|Memphis (Egypt)]]). == Significance == Renutet, identified with the goddess [[:en:Renenutet|Renenutet]], was revered as: * '''"Lady of the Granary"''' * '''"Lady of the Fertile Land"''' * '''"Lady of the Harvest Blessing"''' During the harvest, the Egyptians made offerings to her. '''Stelae found in granaries and field boundaries''' provide evidence of this cult. The '''{{ill|Renenutet Festival|de|Renenutet-Fest|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Renenutet Festival|qq=}}''' was later celebrated on '''{{ill|Pa-en-Renenutet|lt=Pharmouthi|de|Pharmouthi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pa-en-Renenutet|qq=}} 27''', known as the '''"Day of Measuring the [[:en:Barley|barley]]."''' In the '''Theban calendar''', this corresponded to '''{{ill|Pa-en-Chonsu|1=Pa-en-Chonsu|de|Pa-en-Chonsu|1=Pa-en-Chonsu|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pa-en-Chonsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Pa-en-Chonsu|qq=}} 1 (Pachon 1).''' Additionally, as a '''fertility goddess''', Renenutet was credited with ensuring the '''"emergence of flax."''' Because flax was processed into linen, she also bore the titles '''"Lady of the Garments"''' and '''"Overseer of the Temple's Clothing Chamber,"''' referring to the belief that gods were clothed in '''"the garment of Renenutet."''' Records from the '''{{ill|Solar Temple of Niuserre|de|Sonnenheiligtum des Niuserre|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Solar Temple of Niuserre|qq=}}''' indicate that the '''barley harvest''' took place in '''February and March.'''{{ill|Elmar Edel|de|Elmar Edel|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Elmar Edel|qq=}}: ''Zu den Inschriften auf den Jahreszeitenreliefs der „Weltkammer“ aus dem Sonnenheiligtum des Niuserre, Teil 2.'' In: ''Nachrichten der Akademie der Wissenschaften in Göttingen.'' No. 5. Vandenhoeck & Ruprecht, Göttingen 1964, p. 200. == Name Change == The '''first Shemu month''' was later renamed '''{{ill|Pa-en-Chonsu|1=Pa-en-Chonsu|de|Pa-en-Chonsu|1=Pa-en-Chonsu|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pa-en-Chonsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Pa-en-Chonsu|qq=}} (Pachon).''' == See Also == * {{ill|Tebtunis lunar calendar|de|Tebtunis-Mondkalender|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tebtunis lunar calendar|qq=}} == References == * {{ill|Rolf Krauss|de|Rolf Krauss|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rolf Krauss|qq=}}: ''Sothis- und Monddaten: Studien zur astronomischen und technischen Chronologie Altägyptens.'' Gerstenberg, Hildesheim 1985, {{Falsche ISBN|3-8067-8086-X}}. * [[:en:Richard Anthony Parker|Richard Anthony Parker]]: ''The calendars of ancient Egypt.'' Chicago Press, Chicago 1950. * {{ill|Siegfried Schott|de|Siegfried Schott|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Siegfried Schott|qq=}}: ''Altägyptische Festdaten.'' Verlag der Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur, Mainz/ Wiesbaden 1950. == Notes == {{Navigationsleiste Ägyptische Kalendermonate bis zum Anfang des Neuen Reiches}} {{Ancient Egyptian Lunar Months}} [[Category:pages that had Egyptian Calendar Navbar]] {{translated page|de|Renutet|version=|comment=Imported full de history, revisions duplicated and version not here due to an error with my bot related to the German being under the same pagename as the english}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Month of the Ancient Egyptian calendar]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian lunar months]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian festivals]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]][[sn:Renutet]][[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Harvest festivals]] [[Category:Renutet]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Lunar Months]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[sn:Renutet]] [[de:Renutet]] [[ru:%D0%A0%D0%B5%D0%BD%D1%83%D1%82%D0%B5%D1%82]] {{Egyptian Calendar Navbar}}

Wepet-em-asech-ati |NAME3-TRANSKRIPTION = ''Wpt-m-3sḫ-jtj'' |NAME3-ERKLÄRUNG = ''Beginning of the [[:en:Barley|barley]] harvest'' |IMAGE1 = Illustration Hordeum vulgare0.jpg |IMAGE1-WIDTH = 210px }} '''Renutet''' ("The Nourishing One") denoted in the [[:en:Egyptian calendar|Ancient Egyptian calendar]] the beginning of the harvest season and the first month of the [[:en:Season of the Harvest|Schemu]] period as the "nourishing month." From the {{ill|Predynastic Egypt|de|Prädynastik (Ägypten)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Predynastic Egypt|qq=}} until the end of the [[:en:Middle Kingdom of Egypt|Middle Kingdom of Egypt]], Renutet, originally the ninth month of the {{ill|Sothic calendar|1=Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar|de|Mondkalender (Altes Ägypten)|1=Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar#Der ursprüngliche Mondkalender|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sothic calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar|qq=}}, corresponded to the period from early [[:en:February|February]] to early [[:en:March|March]]. == History == [[:en:Alan Gardiner|Alan Gardiner]] and [[:en:Richard Anthony Parker|Richard Anthony Parker]] theorized that Renutet underwent a calendrical shift over time, eventually moving to the eighth month by the [[:en:New Kingdom of Egypt|New Kingdom of Egypt]]. In the {{ill|Ebers calendar|de|Ebers-Kalender|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebers calendar|qq=}}, dated to around '''1517 BCE''', Renutet corresponded to the third [[:en:Season of the Emergence|Peret]] month, spanning from '''February 14 to March 15''' ([[:en:Elephantine|Elephantine]]) and '''February 19 to March 20''' ([[:en:Memphis, Egypt|Memphis (Egypt)]]). == Significance == Renutet, identified with the goddess [[:en:Renenutet|Renenutet]], was revered as: * '''"Lady of the Granary"''' * '''"Lady of the Fertile Land"''' * '''"Lady of the Harvest Blessing"''' During the harvest, the Egyptians made offerings to her. '''Stelae found in granaries and field boundaries''' provide evidence of this cult. The '''{{ill|Renenutet Festival|de|Renenutet-Fest|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Renenutet Festival|qq=}}''' was later celebrated on '''{{ill|Pa-en-Renenutet|lt=Pharmouthi|de|Pharmouthi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pa-en-Renenutet|qq=}} 27''', known as the '''"Day of Measuring the [[:en:Barley|barley]]."''' In the '''Theban calendar''', this corresponded to '''{{ill|Pa-en-Chonsu|1=Pa-en-Chonsu|de|Pa-en-Chonsu|1=Pa-en-Chonsu|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pa-en-Chonsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Pa-en-Chonsu|qq=}} 1 (Pachon 1).''' Additionally, as a '''fertility goddess''', Renenutet was credited with ensuring the '''"emergence of flax."''' Because flax was processed into linen, she also bore the titles '''"Lady of the Garments"''' and '''"Overseer of the Temple's Clothing Chamber,"''' referring to the belief that gods were clothed in '''"the garment of Renenutet."''' Records from the '''{{ill|Solar Temple of Niuserre|de|Sonnenheiligtum des Niuserre|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Solar Temple of Niuserre|qq=}}''' indicate that the '''barley harvest''' took place in '''February and March.'''{{ill|Elmar Edel|de|Elmar Edel|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Elmar Edel|qq=}}: ''Zu den Inschriften auf den Jahreszeitenreliefs der „Weltkammer“ aus dem Sonnenheiligtum des Niuserre, Teil 2.'' In: ''Nachrichten der Akademie der Wissenschaften in Göttingen.'' No. 5. Vandenhoeck & Ruprecht, Göttingen 1964, p. 200. == Name Change == The '''first Shemu month''' was later renamed '''{{ill|Pa-en-Chonsu|1=Pa-en-Chonsu|de|Pa-en-Chonsu|1=Pa-en-Chonsu|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pa-en-Chonsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Pa-en-Chonsu|qq=}} (Pachon).''' == See Also == * {{ill|Tebtunis lunar calendar|de|Tebtunis-Mondkalender|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tebtunis lunar calendar|qq=}} == References == * {{ill|Rolf Krauss|de|Rolf Krauss|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rolf Krauss|qq=}}: ''Sothis- und Monddaten: Studien zur astronomischen und technischen Chronologie Altägyptens.'' Gerstenberg, Hildesheim 1985, {{Falsche ISBN|3-8067-8086-X}}. * [[:en:Richard Anthony Parker|Richard Anthony Parker]]: ''The calendars of ancient Egypt.'' Chicago Press, Chicago 1950. * {{ill|Siegfried Schott|de|Siegfried Schott|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Siegfried Schott|qq=}}: ''Altägyptische Festdaten.'' Verlag der Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur, Mainz/ Wiesbaden 1950. == Notes == {{Navigationsleiste Ägyptische Kalendermonate bis zum Anfang des Neuen Reiches}} {{Ancient Egyptian Lunar Months}} [[Category:pages that had Egyptian Calendar Navbar]] {{translated page|de|Renutet|version=|comment=Imported full de history, revisions duplicated and version not here due to an error with my bot related to the German being under the same pagename as the english}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Month of the Ancient Egyptian calendar]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian lunar months]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian festivals]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]][[sn:Renutet]][[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Harvest festivals]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Lunar Months]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[sn:Renutet]] [[de:Renutet]] [[ru:%D0%A0%D0%B5%D0%BD%D1%83%D1%82%D0%B5%D1%82]] {{Egyptian Calendar Navbar}}

''The Nourishing One'' |NAME2 = N11:Z1 S-N35B
1-nu-schemu |NAME2-TRANSKRIPTION = ''1-nw-šmw'' |NAME2-ERKLÄRUNG = ''First month of the [[:en:Season of the Harvest|Schemu]] period'' |NAME3 = F13:X1-G17-G1-O34:Aa1-M17-M17:X1-M33
Wepet-em-asech-ati |NAME3-TRANSKRIPTION = ''Wpt-m-3sḫ-jtj'' |NAME3-ERKLÄRUNG = ''Beginning of the [[:en:Barley|barley]] harvest'' |IMAGE1 = Illustration Hordeum vulgare0.jpg |IMAGE1-WIDTH = 210px }} '''Renutet''' ("The Nourishing One") denoted in the [[:en:Egyptian calendar|Ancient Egyptian calendar]] the beginning of the harvest season and the first month of the [[:en:Season of the Harvest|Schemu]] period as the "nourishing month." From the {{ill|Predynastic Egypt|de|Prädynastik (Ägypten)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Predynastic Egypt|qq=}} until the end of the [[:en:Middle Kingdom of Egypt|Middle Kingdom of Egypt]], Renutet, originally the ninth month of the {{ill|Sothic calendar|1=Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar|de|Mondkalender (Altes Ägypten)|1=Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar#Der ursprüngliche Mondkalender|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sothic calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar|qq=}}, corresponded to the period from early [[:en:February|February]] to early [[:en:March|March]]. == History == [[:en:Alan Gardiner|Alan Gardiner]] and [[:en:Richard Anthony Parker|Richard Anthony Parker]] theorized that Renutet underwent a calendrical shift over time, eventually moving to the eighth month by the [[:en:New Kingdom of Egypt|New Kingdom of Egypt]]. In the {{ill|Ebers calendar|de|Ebers-Kalender|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebers calendar|qq=}}, dated to around '''1517 BCE''', Renutet corresponded to the third [[:en:Season of the Emergence|Peret]] month, spanning from '''February 14 to March 15''' ([[:en:Elephantine|Elephantine]]) and '''February 19 to March 20''' ([[:en:Memphis, Egypt|Memphis (Egypt)]]). == Significance == Renutet, identified with the goddess [[:en:Renenutet|Renenutet]], was revered as: * '''"Lady of the Granary"''' * '''"Lady of the Fertile Land"''' * '''"Lady of the Harvest Blessing"''' During the harvest, the Egyptians made offerings to her. '''Stelae found in granaries and field boundaries''' provide evidence of this cult. The '''{{ill|Renenutet Festival|de|Renenutet-Fest|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Renenutet Festival|qq=}}''' was later celebrated on '''{{ill|Pa-en-Renenutet|lt=Pharmouthi|de|Pharmouthi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pa-en-Renenutet|qq=}} 27''', known as the '''"Day of Measuring the [[:en:Barley|barley]]."''' In the '''Theban calendar''', this corresponded to '''{{ill|Pa-en-Chonsu|1=Pa-en-Chonsu|de|Pa-en-Chonsu|1=Pa-en-Chonsu|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pa-en-Chonsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Pa-en-Chonsu|qq=}} 1 (Pachon 1).''' Additionally, as a '''fertility goddess''', Renenutet was credited with ensuring the '''"emergence of flax."''' Because flax was processed into linen, she also bore the titles '''"Lady of the Garments"''' and '''"Overseer of the Temple's Clothing Chamber,"''' referring to the belief that gods were clothed in '''"the garment of Renenutet."''' Records from the '''{{ill|Solar Temple of Niuserre|de|Sonnenheiligtum des Niuserre|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Solar Temple of Niuserre|qq=}}''' indicate that the '''barley harvest''' took place in '''February and March.'''{{ill|Elmar Edel|de|Elmar Edel|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Elmar Edel|qq=}}: ''Zu den Inschriften auf den Jahreszeitenreliefs der „Weltkammer“ aus dem Sonnenheiligtum des Niuserre, Teil 2.'' In: ''Nachrichten der Akademie der Wissenschaften in Göttingen.'' No. 5. Vandenhoeck & Ruprecht, Göttingen 1964, p. 200. == Name Change == The '''first Shemu month''' was later renamed '''{{ill|Pa-en-Chonsu|1=Pa-en-Chonsu|de|Pa-en-Chonsu|1=Pa-en-Chonsu|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pa-en-Chonsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Pa-en-Chonsu|qq=}} (Pachon).''' == See Also == * {{ill|Tebtunis lunar calendar|de|Tebtunis-Mondkalender|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tebtunis lunar calendar|qq=}} == References == * {{ill|Rolf Krauss|de|Rolf Krauss|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rolf Krauss|qq=}}: ''Sothis- und Monddaten: Studien zur astronomischen und technischen Chronologie Altägyptens.'' Gerstenberg, Hildesheim 1985, {{Falsche ISBN|3-8067-8086-X}}. * [[:en:Richard Anthony Parker|Richard Anthony Parker]]: ''The calendars of ancient Egypt.'' Chicago Press, Chicago 1950. * {{ill|Siegfried Schott|de|Siegfried Schott|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Siegfried Schott|qq=}}: ''Altägyptische Festdaten.'' Verlag der Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur, Mainz/ Wiesbaden 1950. == Notes == {{Navigationsleiste Ägyptische Kalendermonate bis zum Anfang des Neuen Reiches}} {{Ancient Egyptian Lunar Months}} [[Category:pages that had Egyptian Calendar Navbar]] {{translated page|de|Renutet|version=|comment=Imported full de history, revisions duplicated and version not here due to an error with my bot related to the German being under the same pagename as the english}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Month of the Ancient Egyptian calendar]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian festivals]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]][[sn:Renutet]][[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Harvest festivals]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[sn:Renutet]] [[de:Renutet]] [[ru:%D0%A0%D0%B5%D0%BD%D1%83%D1%82%D0%B5%D1%82]] {{Egyptian Calendar Navbar}} [[Category:Months of the Ancient Egyptian Calendars]] [[ru:Ренутет]]

''Rnwtt''
''The Nourishing One'' |NAME2 = N11:Z1 S-N35B
1-nu-schemu |NAME2-TRANSKRIPTION = ''1-nw-šmw'' |NAME2-ERKLÄRUNG = ''First month of the [[:en:Season of the Harvest|Schemu]] period'' |NAME3 = F13:X1-G17-G1-O34:Aa1-M17-M17:X1-M33
Wepet-em-asech-ati |NAME3-TRANSKRIPTION = ''Wpt-m-3sḫ-jtj'' |NAME3-ERKLÄRUNG = ''Beginning of the [[:en:Barley|barley]] harvest'' |IMAGE1 = Illustration Hordeum vulgare0.jpg |IMAGE1-WIDTH = 210px }} '''Renutet''' ("The Nourishing One") denoted in the [[:en:Egyptian calendar|Ancient Egyptian calendar]] the beginning of the harvest season and the first month of the [[:en:Season of the Harvest|Schemu]] period as the "nourishing month." From the {{ill|Predynastic Egypt|de|Prädynastik (Ägypten)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Predynastic Egypt|qq=}} until the end of the [[:en:Middle Kingdom of Egypt|Middle Kingdom of Egypt]], Renutet, originally the ninth month of the {{ill|Sothic calendar|1=Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar|de|Mondkalender (Altes Ägypten)|1=Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar#Der ursprüngliche Mondkalender|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sothic calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar|qq=}}, corresponded to the period from early [[:en:February|February]] to early [[:en:March|March]]. == History == [[:en:Alan Gardiner|Alan Gardiner]] and [[:en:Richard Anthony Parker|Richard Anthony Parker]] theorized that Renutet underwent a calendrical shift over time, eventually moving to the eighth month by the [[:en:New Kingdom of Egypt|New Kingdom of Egypt]]. In the {{ill|Ebers calendar|de|Ebers-Kalender|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebers calendar|qq=}}, dated to around '''1517 BCE''', Renutet corresponded to the third [[:en:Season of the Emergence|Peret]] month, spanning from '''February 14 to March 15''' ([[:en:Elephantine|Elephantine]]) and '''February 19 to March 20''' ([[:en:Memphis, Egypt|Memphis (Egypt)]]). == Significance == Renutet, identified with the goddess [[:en:Renenutet|Renenutet]], was revered as: * '''"Lady of the Granary"''' * '''"Lady of the Fertile Land"''' * '''"Lady of the Harvest Blessing"''' During the harvest, the Egyptians made offerings to her. '''Stelae found in granaries and field boundaries''' provide evidence of this cult. The '''{{ill|Renenutet Festival|de|Renenutet-Fest|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Renenutet Festival|qq=}}''' was later celebrated on '''{{ill|Pa-en-Renenutet|lt=Pharmouthi|de|Pharmouthi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pa-en-Renenutet|qq=}} 27''', known as the '''"Day of Measuring the [[:en:Barley|barley]]."''' In the '''Theban calendar''', this corresponded to '''{{ill|Pa-en-Chonsu|1=Pa-en-Chonsu|de|Pa-en-Chonsu|1=Pa-en-Chonsu|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pa-en-Chonsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Pa-en-Chonsu|qq=}} 1 (Pachon 1).''' Additionally, as a '''fertility goddess''', Renenutet was credited with ensuring the '''"emergence of flax."''' Because flax was processed into linen, she also bore the titles '''"Lady of the Garments"''' and '''"Overseer of the Temple's Clothing Chamber,"''' referring to the belief that gods were clothed in '''"the garment of Renenutet."''' Records from the '''{{ill|Solar Temple of Niuserre|de|Sonnenheiligtum des Niuserre|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Solar Temple of Niuserre|qq=}}''' indicate that the '''barley harvest''' took place in '''February and March.'''{{ill|Elmar Edel|de|Elmar Edel|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Elmar Edel|qq=}}: ''Zu den Inschriften auf den Jahreszeitenreliefs der „Weltkammer“ aus dem Sonnenheiligtum des Niuserre, Teil 2.'' In: ''Nachrichten der Akademie der Wissenschaften in Göttingen.'' No. 5. Vandenhoeck & Ruprecht, Göttingen 1964, p. 200. == Name Change == The '''first Shemu month''' was later renamed '''{{ill|Pa-en-Chonsu|1=Pa-en-Chonsu|de|Pa-en-Chonsu|1=Pa-en-Chonsu|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pa-en-Chonsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Pa-en-Chonsu|qq=}} (Pachon).''' == See Also == * {{ill|Tebtunis lunar calendar|de|Tebtunis-Mondkalender|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tebtunis lunar calendar|qq=}} == References == * {{ill|Rolf Krauss|de|Rolf Krauss|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rolf Krauss|qq=}}: ''Sothis- und Monddaten: Studien zur astronomischen und technischen Chronologie Altägyptens.'' Gerstenberg, Hildesheim 1985, {{Falsche ISBN|3-8067-8086-X}}. * [[:en:Richard Anthony Parker|Richard Anthony Parker]]: ''The calendars of ancient Egypt.'' Chicago Press, Chicago 1950. * {{ill|Siegfried Schott|de|Siegfried Schott|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Siegfried Schott|qq=}}: ''Altägyptische Festdaten.'' Verlag der Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur, Mainz/ Wiesbaden 1950. == Notes == {{Navigationsleiste Ägyptische Kalendermonate bis zum Anfang des Neuen Reiches}} {{Ancient Egyptian Lunar Months}} {{translated page|de|Renutet|version=|comment=Imported full de history, revisions duplicated and version not here due to an error with my bot related to the German being under the same pagename as the english}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Months of the Ancient Egyptian Calendars]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian festivals]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]][[sn:Renutet]][[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Harvest festivals]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[sn:Renutet]] [[de:Renutet]] [[ru:%D0%A0%D0%B5%D0%BD%D1%83%D1%82%D0%B5%D1%82]] {{Egyptian Calendar Navbar}} [[Category:Months of the Ancient Egyptian Calendars]] [[ru:Ренутет]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Renutet]][[Category:qq]]
