''Rnwtt''
''The Nourishing One'' |NAME2 = N11:Z1 S-N35B
1-nu-schemu |NAME2-TRANSKRIPTION = ''1-nw-šmw'' |NAME2-ERKLÄRUNG = ''First month of the [[:en:Season of the Harvest|Schemu]] period'' |NAME3 = F13:X1-G17-G1-O34:Aa1-M17-M17:X1-M33
Wepet-em-asech-ati |NAME3-TRANSKRIPTION = ''Wpt-m-3sḫ-jtj'' |NAME3-ERKLÄRUNG = ''Beginning of the [[:en:Barley|barley]] harvest'' |IMAGE1 = Illustration Hordeum vulgare0.jpg |IMAGE1-WIDTH = 210px }} '''Renutet''' ("The Nourishing One") denoted in the [[:en:Egyptian calendar|Ancient Egyptian calendar]] the beginning of the harvest season and the first month of the [[:en:Season of the Harvest|Schemu]] period as the "nourishing month." From the {{ill|Predynastic Egypt|de|Prädynastik (Ägypten)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Predynastic Egypt|qq=}} until the end of the [[:en:Middle Kingdom of Egypt|Middle Kingdom of Egypt]], Renutet, originally the ninth month of the {{ill|Sothic calendar|1=Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar|de|Mondkalender (Altes Ägypten)|1=Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar#Der ursprüngliche Mondkalender|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sothic calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar|qq=}}, corresponded to the period from early [[:en:February|February]] to early [[:en:March|March]]. == History == [[:en:Alan Gardiner|Alan Gardiner]] and [[:en:Richard Anthony Parker|Richard Anthony Parker]] theorized that Renutet underwent a calendrical shift over time, eventually moving to the eighth month by the [[:en:New Kingdom of Egypt|New Kingdom of Egypt]]. In the {{ill|Ebers calendar|de|Ebers-Kalender|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebers calendar|qq=}}, dated to around '''1517 BCE''', Renutet corresponded to the third [[:en:Season of the Emergence|Peret]] month, spanning from '''February 14 to March 15''' ([[:en:Elephantine|Elephantine]]) and '''February 19 to March 20''' ([[:en:Memphis, Egypt|Memphis (Egypt)]]). == Significance == Renutet, identified with the goddess [[:en:Renenutet|Renenutet]], was revered as: * '''"Lady of the Granary"''' * '''"Lady of the Fertile Land"''' * '''"Lady of the Harvest Blessing"''' During the harvest, the Egyptians made offerings to her. '''Stelae found in granaries and field boundaries''' provide evidence of this cult. The '''{{ill|Renenutet Festival|de|Renenutet-Fest|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Renenutet Festival|qq=}}''' was later celebrated on '''{{ill|Pa-en-Renenutet|lt=Pharmouthi|de|Pharmouthi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pa-en-Renenutet|qq=}} 27''', known as the '''"Day of Measuring the [[:en:Barley|barley]]."''' In the '''Theban calendar''', this corresponded to '''{{ill|Pa-en-Chonsu|1=Pa-en-Chonsu|de|Pa-en-Chonsu|1=Pa-en-Chonsu|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pa-en-Chonsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Pa-en-Chonsu|qq=}} 1 (Pachon 1).''' Additionally, as a '''fertility goddess''', Renenutet was credited with ensuring the '''"emergence of flax."''' Because flax was processed into linen, she also bore the titles '''"Lady of the Garments"''' and '''"Overseer of the Temple's Clothing Chamber,"''' referring to the belief that gods were clothed in '''"the garment of Renenutet."''' Records from the '''{{ill|Solar Temple of Niuserre|de|Sonnenheiligtum des Niuserre|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Solar Temple of Niuserre|qq=}}''' indicate that the '''barley harvest''' took place in '''February and March.'''{{ill|Elmar Edel|de|Elmar Edel|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Elmar Edel|qq=}}: ''Zu den Inschriften auf den Jahreszeitenreliefs der „Weltkammer“ aus dem Sonnenheiligtum des Niuserre, Teil 2.'' In: ''Nachrichten der Akademie der Wissenschaften in Göttingen.'' No. 5. Vandenhoeck & Ruprecht, Göttingen 1964, p. 200. == Name Change == The '''first Shemu month''' was later renamed '''{{ill|Pa-en-Chonsu|1=Pa-en-Chonsu|de|Pa-en-Chonsu|1=Pa-en-Chonsu|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pa-en-Chonsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Pa-en-Chonsu|qq=}} (Pachon).''' == See Also == * {{ill|Tebtunis lunar calendar|de|Tebtunis-Mondkalender|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tebtunis lunar calendar|qq=}} == References == * {{ill|Rolf Krauss|de|Rolf Krauss|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rolf Krauss|qq=}}: ''Sothis- und Monddaten: Studien zur astronomischen und technischen Chronologie Altägyptens.'' Gerstenberg, Hildesheim 1985, {{Falsche ISBN|3-8067-8086-X}}. * [[:en:Richard Anthony Parker|Richard Anthony Parker]]: ''The calendars of ancient Egypt.'' Chicago Press, Chicago 1950. * {{ill|Siegfried Schott|de|Siegfried Schott|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Siegfried Schott|qq=}}: ''Altägyptische Festdaten.'' Verlag der Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur, Mainz/ Wiesbaden 1950. == Notes == {{Navigationsleiste Ägyptische Kalendermonate bis zum Anfang des Neuen Reiches}} {{Ancient Egyptian Lunar Months}} [[Category:Translated page but missing wikidata]]{{translated page|de|Renutet|version=|comment=Imported full de history, revisions duplicated and version not here due to an error with my bot related to the German being under the same pagename as the english}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Months of the Ancient Egyptian Calendars]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian festivals]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]][[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Harvest festivals]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[de:Renutet]] [[ru:%D0%A0%D0%B5%D0%BD%D1%83%D1%82%D0%B5%D1%82]] {{Egyptian Calendar Navbar}} [[Category:Months of the Ancient Egyptian Calendars]] [[ru:Ренутет]] [[Category:Missing wikidata]]

''Rnwtt''
''The Nourishing One'' |NAME2 = N11:Z1 S-N35B
1-nu-schemu |NAME2-TRANSKRIPTION = ''1-nw-šmw'' |NAME2-ERKLÄRUNG = ''First month of the [[:en:Season of the Harvest|Schemu]] period'' |NAME3 = F13:X1-G17-G1-O34:Aa1-M17-M17:X1-M33
Wepet-em-asech-ati |NAME3-TRANSKRIPTION = ''Wpt-m-3sḫ-jtj'' |NAME3-ERKLÄRUNG = ''Beginning of the [[:en:Barley|barley]] harvest'' |IMAGE1 = Illustration Hordeum vulgare0.jpg |IMAGE1-WIDTH = 210px }} '''Renutet''' ("The Nourishing One") denoted in the [[:en:Egyptian calendar|Ancient Egyptian calendar]] the beginning of the harvest season and the first month of the [[:en:Season of the Harvest|Schemu]] period as the "nourishing month." From the {{ill|Predynastic Egypt|de|Prädynastik (Ägypten)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Predynastic Egypt|qq=|WD=Q714601}} until the end of the [[:en:Middle Kingdom of Egypt|Middle Kingdom of Egypt]], Renutet, originally the ninth month of the {{ill|Sothic calendar|1=Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar|de|Mondkalender (Altes Ägypten)|1=Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar#Der ursprüngliche Mondkalender|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sothic calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar|qq=|WD=Q1944246}}, corresponded to the period from early [[:en:February|February]] to early [[:en:March|March]]. == History == [[:en:Alan Gardiner|Alan Gardiner]] and [[:en:Richard Anthony Parker|Richard Anthony Parker]] theorized that Renutet underwent a calendrical shift over time, eventually moving to the eighth month by the [[:en:New Kingdom of Egypt|New Kingdom of Egypt]]. In the {{ill|Ebers calendar|de|Ebers-Kalender|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebers calendar|qq=|WD=Q1279717}}, dated to around '''1517 BCE''', Renutet corresponded to the third [[:en:Season of the Emergence|Peret]] month, spanning from '''February 14 to March 15''' ([[:en:Elephantine|Elephantine]]) and '''February 19 to March 20''' ([[:en:Memphis, Egypt|Memphis (Egypt)]]). == Significance == Renutet, identified with the goddess [[:en:Renenutet|Renenutet]], was revered as: * '''"Lady of the Granary"''' * '''"Lady of the Fertile Land"''' * '''"Lady of the Harvest Blessing"''' During the harvest, the Egyptians made offerings to her. '''Stelae found in granaries and field boundaries''' provide evidence of this cult. The '''{{ill|Renenutet Festival|de|Renenutet-Fest|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Renenutet Festival|qq=|comment=no wikipedias work}}''' was later celebrated on '''{{ill|Pa-en-Renenutet|lt=Pharmouthi|de|Pharmouthi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pa-en-Renenutet|qq=|WD=Q846942}} 27''', known as the '''"Day of Measuring the [[:en:Barley|barley]]."''' In the '''Theban calendar''', this corresponded to '''{{ill|Pa-en-Chonsu|1=Pa-en-Chonsu|de|Pa-en-Chonsu|1=Pa-en-Chonsu|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pa-en-Chonsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Pa-en-Chonsu|qq=|WD=Q56414619}} 1 (Pachon 1).''' Additionally, as a '''fertility goddess''', Renenutet was credited with ensuring the '''"emergence of flax."''' Because flax was processed into linen, she also bore the titles '''"Lady of the Garments"''' and '''"Overseer of the Temple's Clothing Chamber,"''' referring to the belief that gods were clothed in '''"the garment of Renenutet."''' Records from the '''{{ill|Solar Temple of Niuserre|de|Sonnenheiligtum des Niuserre|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Solar Temple of Niuserre|qq=|WD=Q2009417}}''' indicate that the '''barley harvest''' took place in '''February and March.'''{{ill|Elmar Edel|de|Elmar Edel|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Elmar Edel|qq=|WD=Q105633}}: ''Zu den Inschriften auf den Jahreszeitenreliefs der „Weltkammer“ aus dem Sonnenheiligtum des Niuserre, Teil 2.'' In: ''Nachrichten der Akademie der Wissenschaften in Göttingen.'' No. 5. Vandenhoeck & Ruprecht, Göttingen 1964, p. 200. == Name Change == The '''first Shemu month''' was later renamed '''{{ill|Pa-en-Chonsu|1=Pa-en-Chonsu|de|Pa-en-Chonsu|1=Pa-en-Chonsu|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pa-en-Chonsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Pa-en-Chonsu|qq=|WD=Q56414619}} (Pachon).''' == See Also == * {{ill|Tebtunis lunar calendar|de|Tebtunis-Mondkalender|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tebtunis lunar calendar|qq=|WD=Q1458800}} == References == * {{ill|Rolf Krauss|de|Rolf Krauss|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rolf Krauss|qq=|WD=Q91239}}: ''Sothis- und Monddaten: Studien zur astronomischen und technischen Chronologie Altägyptens.'' Gerstenberg, Hildesheim 1985, {{Falsche ISBN|3-8067-8086-X}}. * [[:en:Richard Anthony Parker|Richard Anthony Parker]]: ''The calendars of ancient Egypt.'' Chicago Press, Chicago 1950. * {{ill|Siegfried Schott|de|Siegfried Schott|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Siegfried Schott|qq=|WD=Q102228}}: ''Altägyptische Festdaten.'' Verlag der Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur, Mainz/ Wiesbaden 1950. == Notes == {{Navigationsleiste Ägyptische Kalendermonate bis zum Anfang des Neuen Reiches}} {{Ancient Egyptian Lunar Months}} {{translated page|de|Renutet|version=|comment=Imported full de history, revisions duplicated and version not here due to an error with my bot related to the German being under the same pagename as the english}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Months of the Ancient Egyptian Calendars]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian festivals]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]][[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Harvest festivals]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[de:Renutet]] [[ru:%D0%A0%D0%B5%D0%BD%D1%83%D1%82%D0%B5%D1%82]] {{Egyptian Calendar Navbar}} [[Category:Months of the Ancient Egyptian Calendars]] [[ru:Ренутет]] {{wikidata link|Q132171870}}

[[de:Renutet]][[ru:Ренутет]] {{Infobox hieroglyphs |NAME = r:n-Z7-t:t |NAME-TRANSKRIPTION = |NAME-ERKLÄRUNG = [[:en:Renenutet|Renenutet]]
''Rnwtt''
''The Nourishing One'' |NAME2 = N11:Z1 S-N35B
1-nu-schemu |NAME2-TRANSKRIPTION = ''1-nw-šmw'' |NAME2-ERKLÄRUNG = ''First month of the [[:en:Season of the Harvest|Schemu]] period'' |NAME3 = F13:X1-G17-G1-O34:Aa1-M17-M17:X1-M33
Wepet-em-asech-ati |NAME3-TRANSKRIPTION = ''Wpt-m-3sḫ-jtj'' |NAME3-ERKLÄRUNG = ''Beginning of the [[:en:Barley|barley]] harvest'' |IMAGE1 = Illustration Hordeum vulgare0.jpg |IMAGE1-WIDTH = 210px }} '''Renutet''' ("The Nourishing One") denoted in the [[:en:Egyptian calendar|Ancient Egyptian calendar]] the beginning of the harvest season and the first month of the [[:en:Season of the Harvest|Schemu]] period as the "nourishing month." From the {{ill|Predynastic Egypt|de|Prädynastik (Ägypten)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Predynastic Egypt|qq=|WD=Q714601}} until the end of the [[:en:Middle Kingdom of Egypt|Middle Kingdom of Egypt]], Renutet, originally the ninth month of the {{ill|Sothic calendar|1=Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar|de|Mondkalender (Altes Ägypten)|1=Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar#Der ursprüngliche Mondkalender|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sothic calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar|qq=|WD=Q1944246}}, corresponded to the period from early [[:en:February|February]] to early [[:en:March|March]]. == History == [[:en:Alan Gardiner|Alan Gardiner]] and [[:en:Richard Anthony Parker|Richard Anthony Parker]] theorized that Renutet underwent a calendrical shift over time, eventually moving to the eighth month by the [[:en:New Kingdom of Egypt|New Kingdom of Egypt]]. In the {{ill|Ebers calendar|de|Ebers-Kalender|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebers calendar|qq=|WD=Q1279717}}, dated to around '''1517 BCE''', Renutet corresponded to the third [[:en:Season of the Emergence|Peret]] month, spanning from '''February 14 to March 15''' ([[:en:Elephantine|Elephantine]]) and '''February 19 to March 20''' ([[:en:Memphis, Egypt|Memphis (Egypt)]]). == Significance == Renutet, identified with the goddess [[:en:Renenutet|Renenutet]], was revered as: * '''"Lady of the Granary"''' * '''"Lady of the Fertile Land"''' * '''"Lady of the Harvest Blessing"''' During the harvest, the Egyptians made offerings to her. '''Stelae found in granaries and field boundaries''' provide evidence of this cult. The '''{{ill|Renenutet Festival|de|Renenutet-Fest|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Renenutet Festival|qq=|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}}''' was later celebrated on '''{{ill|Pa-en-Renenutet|lt=Pharmouthi|de|Pharmouthi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pa-en-Renenutet|qq=|WD=Q846942}} 27''', known as the '''"Day of Measuring the [[:en:Barley|barley]]."''' In the '''Theban calendar''', this corresponded to '''{{ill|Pa-en-Chonsu|1=Pa-en-Chonsu|de|Pa-en-Chonsu|1=Pa-en-Chonsu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pa-en-Chonsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Pa-en-Chonsu|qq=|WD=Q56414619}} 1 (Pachon 1).''' Additionally, as a '''fertility goddess''', Renenutet was credited with ensuring the '''"emergence of flax."''' Because flax was processed into linen, she also bore the titles '''"Lady of the Garments"''' and '''"Overseer of the Temple's Clothing Chamber,"''' referring to the belief that gods were clothed in '''"the garment of Renenutet."''' Records from the '''{{ill|Solar Temple of Niuserre|de|Sonnenheiligtum des Niuserre|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Solar Temple of Niuserre|qq=|WD=Q2009417}}''' indicate that the '''barley harvest''' took place in '''February and March.'''{{ill|Elmar Edel|de|Elmar Edel|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Elmar Edel|qq=|WD=Q105633}}: ''Zu den Inschriften auf den Jahreszeitenreliefs der „Weltkammer“ aus dem Sonnenheiligtum des Niuserre, Teil 2.'' In: ''Nachrichten der Akademie der Wissenschaften in Göttingen.'' No. 5. Vandenhoeck & Ruprecht, Göttingen 1964, p. 200. == Name Change == The '''first Shemu month''' was later renamed '''{{ill|Pa-en-Chonsu|1=Pa-en-Chonsu|de|Pa-en-Chonsu|1=Pa-en-Chonsu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pa-en-Chonsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Pa-en-Chonsu|qq=|WD=Q56414619}} (Pachon).''' == See Also == * {{ill|Tebtunis lunar calendar|de|Tebtunis-Mondkalender|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tebtunis lunar calendar|qq=|WD=Q1458800}} == References == * {{ill|Rolf Krauss|de|Rolf Krauss|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rolf Krauss|qq=|WD=Q91239}}: ''Sothis- und Monddaten: Studien zur astronomischen und technischen Chronologie Altägyptens.'' Gerstenberg, Hildesheim 1985, {{Falsche ISBN|3-8067-8086-X}}. * [[:en:Richard Anthony Parker|Richard Anthony Parker]]: ''The calendars of ancient Egypt.'' Chicago Press, Chicago 1950. * {{ill|Siegfried Schott|de|Siegfried Schott|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Siegfried Schott|qq=|WD=Q102228}}: ''Altägyptische Festdaten.'' Verlag der Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur, Mainz/ Wiesbaden 1950. == Notes == {{Navigationsleiste Ägyptische Kalendermonate bis zum Anfang des Neuen Reiches}} {{Ancient Egyptian Lunar Months}} {{translated page|de|Renutet|version=|comment=Imported full de history, revisions duplicated and version not here due to an error with my bot related to the German being under the same pagename as the english}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Months of the Ancient Egyptian Calendars]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian festivals]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]][[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Harvest festivals]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[de:Renutet]] [[ru:%D0%A0%D0%B5%D0%BD%D1%83%D1%82%D0%B5%D1%82]] {{Egyptian Calendar Navbar}} [[Category:Months of the Ancient Egyptian Calendars]] [[ru:Ренутет]] {{wikidata link|Q132171870}}

In the '''Theban calendar''', this corresponded to '''{{ill|Pa-en-Chonsu|1=Pa-en-Chonsu|de|Pa-en-Chonsu|1=Pa-en-Chonsu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pa-en-Chonsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Pa-en-Chonsu|qq=|WD=Q56414619}} 1 (Pachon 1).''' Because flax was processed into linen, she also bore the titles '''"Lady of the Garments"''' and '''"Overseer of the Temple's Clothing Chamber,"''' referring to the belief that gods were clothed in '''"the garment of Renenutet."''' Records from the '''{{ill|Solar Temple of Niuserre|de|Sonnenheiligtum des Niuserre|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Solar Temple of Niuserre|qq=|WD=Q2009417}}''' indicate that the '''barley harvest''' took place in '''February and March.'''{{ill|Elmar Edel|de|Elmar Edel|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Elmar Edel|qq=|WD=Q105633}}: ''Zu den Inschriften auf den Jahreszeitenreliefs der „Weltkammer“ aus dem Sonnenheiligtum des Niuserre, Teil 2.'' Vandenhoeck & Ruprecht, Göttingen 1964, p. 200. == Name Change == The '''first Shemu month''' was later renamed '''{{ill|Pa-en-Chonsu|1=Pa-en-Chonsu|de|Pa-en-Chonsu|1=Pa-en-Chonsu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pa-en-Chonsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Pa-en-Chonsu|qq=|WD=Q56414619}} (Pachon).''' == See Also == * {{ill|Tebtunis lunar calendar|de|Tebtunis-Mondkalender|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tebtunis lunar calendar|qq=|WD=Q1458800}} == References == * {{ill|Rolf Krauss|de|Rolf Krauss|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rolf Krauss|qq=|WD=Q91239}}: ''Sothis- und Monddaten: Studien zur astronomischen und technischen Chronologie Altägyptens.'' Verlag der Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur, Mainz/ Wiesbaden 1950. == Notes == {{Navigationsleiste Ägyptische Kalendermonate bis zum Anfang des Neuen Reiches}} {{Ancient Egyptian Lunar Months}} {{translated page|de|Renutet|version=|comment=Imported full de history, revisions duplicated and version not here due to an error with my bot related to the German being under the same pagename as the english}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Months of the Ancient Egyptian Calendars]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian festivals]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]][[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Harvest festivals]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[de:Renutet]] [[ru:%D0%A0%D0%B5%D0%BD%D1%83%D1%82%D0%B5%D1%82]] {{Egyptian Calendar Navbar}} [[Category:Months of the Ancient Egyptian Calendars]] [[ru:Ренутет]] {{wikidata link|Q132171870}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata no English label]]

[[de:Renutet]][[ru:Ренутет]] {{Infobox hieroglyphs |NAME = r:n-Z7-t:t |NAME-TRANSKRIPTION = |NAME-ERKLÄRUNG = [[:en:Renenutet|Renenutet]]
''Rnwtt''
''The Nourishing One'' |NAME2 = N11:Z1 S-N35B
1-nu-schemu |NAME2-TRANSKRIPTION = ''1-nw-šmw'' |NAME2-ERKLÄRUNG = ''First month of the [[:en:Season of the Harvest|Schemu]] period'' |NAME3 = F13:X1-G17-G1-O34:Aa1-M17-M17:X1-M33
Wepet-em-asech-ati |NAME3-TRANSKRIPTION = ''Wpt-m-3sḫ-jtj'' |NAME3-ERKLÄRUNG = ''Beginning of the [[:en:Barley|barley]] harvest'' |IMAGE1 = Illustration Hordeum vulgare0.jpg |IMAGE1-WIDTH = 210px }} '''Renutet''' ("The Nourishing One") denoted in the [[:en:Egyptian calendar|Ancient Egyptian calendar]] the beginning of the harvest season and the first month of the [[:en:Season of the Harvest|Schemu]] period as the "nourishing month." From the {{ill|Predynastic Egypt|de|Prädynastik (Ägypten)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Predynastic Egypt|qq=|WD=Q714601}} until the end of the [[:en:Middle Kingdom of Egypt|Middle Kingdom of Egypt]], Renutet, originally the ninth month of the {{ill|Sothic calendar|1=Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar|de|Mondkalender (Altes Ägypten)|1=Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar#Der ursprüngliche Mondkalender|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sothic calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar|qq=|WD=Q1944246}}, corresponded to the period from early [[:en:February|February]] to early [[:en:March|March]]. == History == [[:en:Alan Gardiner|Alan Gardiner]] and [[:en:Richard Anthony Parker|Richard Anthony Parker]] theorized that Renutet underwent a calendrical shift over time, eventually moving to the eighth month by the [[:en:New Kingdom of Egypt|New Kingdom of Egypt]]. In the {{ill|Ebers calendar|de|Ebers-Kalender|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebers calendar|qq=|WD=Q1279717}}, dated to around '''1517 BCE''', Renutet corresponded to the third [[:en:Season of the Emergence|Peret]] month, spanning from '''February 14 to March 15''' ([[:en:Elephantine|Elephantine]]) and '''February 19 to March 20''' ([[:en:Memphis, Egypt|Memphis (Egypt)]]). == Significance == Renutet, identified with the goddess [[:en:Renenutet|Renenutet]], was revered as: * '''"Lady of the Granary"''' * '''"Lady of the Fertile Land"''' * '''"Lady of the Harvest Blessing"''' During the harvest, the Egyptians made offerings to her. '''Stelae found in granaries and field boundaries''' provide evidence of this cult. The '''{{ill|Renenutet Festival|de|Renenutet-Fest|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Renenutet Festival|qq=|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}}''' was later celebrated on '''{{ill|Pa-en-Renenutet|lt=Pharmouthi|de|Pharmouthi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pa-en-Renenutet|qq=|WD=Q846942}} 27''', known as the '''"Day of Measuring the [[:en:Barley|barley]]."''' In the '''Theban calendar''', this corresponded to '''{{ill|Pa-en-Chonsu|1=Pa-en-Chonsu|de|Pa-en-Chonsu|1=Pa-en-Chonsu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pa-en-Chonsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Pa-en-Chonsu|qq=|WD=Q56414619}} 1 (Pachon 1).''' Additionally, as a '''fertility goddess''', Renenutet was credited with ensuring the '''"emergence of flax."''' Because flax was processed into linen, she also bore the titles '''"Lady of the Garments"''' and '''"Overseer of the Temple's Clothing Chamber,"''' referring to the belief that gods were clothed in '''"the garment of Renenutet."''' Records from the '''{{ill|Solar Temple of Niuserre|de|Sonnenheiligtum des Niuserre|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Solar Temple of Niuserre|qq=|WD=Q2009417}}''' indicate that the '''barley harvest''' took place in '''February and March.'''{{ill|Elmar Edel|de|Elmar Edel|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Elmar Edel|qq=|WD=Q105633}}: ''Zu den Inschriften auf den Jahreszeitenreliefs der „Weltkammer“ aus dem Sonnenheiligtum des Niuserre, Teil 2.'' In: ''Nachrichten der Akademie der Wissenschaften in Göttingen.'' No. 5. Vandenhoeck & Ruprecht, Göttingen 1964, p. 200. == Name Change == The '''first Shemu month''' was later renamed '''{{ill|Pa-en-Chonsu|1=Pa-en-Chonsu|de|Pa-en-Chonsu|1=Pa-en-Chonsu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pa-en-Chonsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Pa-en-Chonsu|qq=|WD=Q56414619}} (Pachon).''' == See Also == * {{ill|Tebtunis lunar calendar|de|Tebtunis-Mondkalender|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tebtunis lunar calendar|qq=|WD=Q1458800}} == References == * {{ill|Rolf Krauss|de|Rolf Krauss|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rolf Krauss|qq=|WD=Q91239}}: ''Sothis- und Monddaten: Studien zur astronomischen und technischen Chronologie Altägyptens.'' Gerstenberg, Hildesheim 1985, {{Falsche ISBN|3-8067-8086-X}}. * [[:en:Richard Anthony Parker|Richard Anthony Parker]]: ''The calendars of ancient Egypt.'' Chicago Press, Chicago 1950. * {{ill|Siegfried Schott|de|Siegfried Schott|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Siegfried Schott|qq=|WD=Q102228}}: ''Altägyptische Festdaten.'' Verlag der Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur, Mainz/ Wiesbaden 1950. == Notes == {{Navigationsleiste Ägyptische Kalendermonate bis zum Anfang des Neuen Reiches}} {{Ancient Egyptian Lunar Months}} {{translated page|de|Renutet|version=|comment=Imported full de history, revisions duplicated and version not here due to an error with my bot related to the German being under the same pagename as the english}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Months of the Ancient Egyptian Calendars]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian festivals]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]][[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Harvest festivals]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[de:Renutet]] [[ru:%D0%A0%D0%B5%D0%BD%D1%83%D1%82%D0%B5%D1%82]] {{Egyptian Calendar Navbar}} [[Category:Months of the Ancient Egyptian Calendars]] [[ru:Ренутет]] {{wikidata link|Q132171870}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

Vandenhoeck & Ruprecht, Göttingen 1964, p. 200. == Name Change == The '''first Shemu month''' was later renamed '''{{ill|Pa-en-Chonsu|1=Pa-en-Chonsu|de|Pa-en-Chonsu|1=Pa-en-Chonsu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pa-en-Chonsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Pa-en-Chonsu|qq=|WD=Q56414619}} (Pachon).''' == See Also == * {{ill|Tebtunis lunar calendar|de|Tebtunis-Mondkalender|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tebtunis lunar calendar|qq=|WD=Q1458800}} == References == * {{ill|Rolf Krauss|de|Rolf Krauss|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rolf Krauss|qq=|WD=Q91239}}: ''Sothis- und Monddaten: Studien zur astronomischen und technischen Chronologie Altägyptens.'' Gerstenberg, Hildesheim 1985, {{Falsche ISBN|3-8067-8086-X}}. * [[:en:Richard Anthony Parker|Richard Anthony Parker]]: ''The calendars of ancient Egypt.'' Chicago Press, Chicago 1950. * {{ill|Siegfried Schott|de|Siegfried Schott|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Siegfried Schott|qq=|WD=Q102228}}: ''Altägyptische Festdaten.'' Verlag der Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur, Mainz/ Wiesbaden 1950. == Notes == {{Navigationsleiste Ägyptische Kalendermonate bis zum Anfang des Neuen Reiches}} {{Ancient Egyptian Lunar Months}} {{translated page|de|Renutet|version=|comment=Imported full de history, revisions duplicated and version not here due to an error with my bot related to the German being under the same pagename as the english}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Months of the Ancient Egyptian Calendars]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian festivals]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]][[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Harvest festivals]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[de:Renutet]] [[ru:%D0%A0%D0%B5%D0%BD%D1%83%D1%82%D0%B5%D1%82]] {{Egyptian Calendar Navbar}} [[Category:Months of the Ancient Egyptian Calendars]] [[ru:Ренутет]] {{wikidata link|Q132171870}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

[[de:Renutet]][[ru:Ренутет]] {{Infobox hieroglyphs |NAME = r:n-Z7-t:t |NAME-TRANSKRIPTION = |NAME-ERKLÄRUNG = [[:en:Renenutet|Renenutet]]
''Rnwtt''
''The Nourishing One'' |NAME2 = N11:Z1 S-N35B
1-nu-schemu |NAME2-TRANSKRIPTION = ''1-nw-šmw'' |NAME2-ERKLÄRUNG = ''First month of the [[:en:Season of the Harvest|Schemu]] period'' |NAME3 = F13:X1-G17-G1-O34:Aa1-M17-M17:X1-M33
Wepet-em-asech-ati |NAME3-TRANSKRIPTION = ''Wpt-m-3sḫ-jtj'' |NAME3-ERKLÄRUNG = ''Beginning of the [[:en:Barley|barley]] harvest'' |IMAGE1 = Illustration Hordeum vulgare0.jpg |IMAGE1-WIDTH = 210px }} '''Renutet''' ("The Nourishing One") denoted in the [[:en:Egyptian calendar|Ancient Egyptian calendar]] the beginning of the harvest season and the first month of the [[:en:Season of the Harvest|Schemu]] period as the "nourishing month." From the {{ill|Predynastic Egypt|de|Prädynastik (Ägypten)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Predynastic Egypt|qq=|WD=Q714601}} until the end of the [[:en:Middle Kingdom of Egypt|Middle Kingdom of Egypt]], Renutet, originally the ninth month of the {{ill|Sothic calendar|1=Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar|de|Mondkalender (Altes Ägypten)|1=Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar#Der ursprüngliche Mondkalender|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sothic calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar|qq=|WD=Q1944246}}, corresponded to the period from early [[:en:February|February]] to early [[:en:March|March]]. == History == [[:en:Alan Gardiner|Alan Gardiner]] and [[:en:Richard Anthony Parker|Richard Anthony Parker]] theorized that Renutet underwent a calendrical shift over time, eventually moving to the eighth month by the [[:en:New Kingdom of Egypt|New Kingdom of Egypt]]. In the {{ill|Ebers calendar|de|Ebers-Kalender|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebers calendar|qq=|WD=Q1279717}}, dated to around '''1517 BCE''', Renutet corresponded to the third [[:en:Season of the Emergence|Peret]] month, spanning from '''February 14 to March 15''' ([[:en:Elephantine|Elephantine]]) and '''February 19 to March 20''' ([[:en:Memphis, Egypt|Memphis (Egypt)]]). == Significance == Renutet, identified with the goddess [[:en:Renenutet|Renenutet]], was revered as: * '''"Lady of the Granary"''' * '''"Lady of the Fertile Land"''' * '''"Lady of the Harvest Blessing"''' During the harvest, the Egyptians made offerings to her. '''Stelae found in granaries and field boundaries''' provide evidence of this cult. The '''{{ill|Renenutet Festival|de|Renenutet-Fest|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Renenutet Festival|qq=|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}}''' was later celebrated on '''{{ill|Pa-en-Renenutet|lt=Pharmouthi|de|Pharmouthi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pa-en-Renenutet|qq=|WD=Q846942}} 27''', known as the '''"Day of Measuring the [[:en:Barley|barley]]."''' In the '''Theban calendar''', this corresponded to '''{{ill|Pa-en-Chonsu|1=Pa-en-Chonsu|de|Pa-en-Chonsu|1=Pa-en-Chonsu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pa-en-Chonsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Pa-en-Chonsu|qq=|WD=Q56414619}} 1 (Pachon 1).''' Additionally, as a '''fertility goddess''', Renenutet was credited with ensuring the '''"emergence of flax."''' Because flax was processed into linen, she also bore the titles '''"Lady of the Garments"''' and '''"Overseer of the Temple's Clothing Chamber,"''' referring to the belief that gods were clothed in '''"the garment of Renenutet."''' Records from the '''{{ill|Solar Temple of Niuserre|de|Sonnenheiligtum des Niuserre|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Solar Temple of Niuserre|qq=|WD=Q2009417}}''' indicate that the '''barley harvest''' took place in '''February and March.'''{{ill|Elmar Edel|de|Elmar Edel|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Elmar Edel|qq=|WD=Q105633}}: ''Zu den Inschriften auf den Jahreszeitenreliefs der „Weltkammer“ aus dem Sonnenheiligtum des Niuserre, Teil 2.'' In: ''Nachrichten der Akademie der Wissenschaften in Göttingen.'' No. 5. Vandenhoeck & Ruprecht, Göttingen 1964, p. 200. == Name Change == The '''first Shemu month''' was later renamed '''{{ill|Pa-en-Chonsu|1=Pa-en-Chonsu|de|Pa-en-Chonsu|1=Pa-en-Chonsu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pa-en-Chonsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Pa-en-Chonsu|qq=|WD=Q56414619}} (Pachon).''' == See Also == * {{ill|Tebtunis lunar calendar|de|Tebtunis-Mondkalender|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tebtunis lunar calendar|qq=|WD=Q1458800}} == References == * {{ill|Rolf Krauss|de|Rolf Krauss|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rolf Krauss|qq=|WD=Q91239}}: ''Sothis- und Monddaten: Studien zur astronomischen und technischen Chronologie Altägyptens.'' Gerstenberg, Hildesheim 1985, {{Falsche ISBN|3-8067-8086-X}}. * [[:en:Richard Anthony Parker|Richard Anthony Parker]]: ''The calendars of ancient Egypt.'' Chicago Press, Chicago 1950. * {{ill|Siegfried Schott|de|Siegfried Schott|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Siegfried Schott|qq=|WD=Q102228}}: ''Altägyptische Festdaten.'' Verlag der Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur, Mainz/ Wiesbaden 1950. == Notes == {{Navigationsleiste Ägyptische Kalendermonate bis zum Anfang des Neuen Reiches}} {{Ancient Egyptian Lunar Months}} {{translated page|de|Renutet|version=|comment=Imported full de history, revisions duplicated and version not here due to an error with my bot related to the German being under the same pagename as the english}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Months of the Ancient Egyptian Calendars]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian festivals]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]][[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Harvest festivals]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[de:Renutet]] [[ru:%D0%A0%D0%B5%D0%BD%D1%83%D1%82%D0%B5%D1%82]] {{Egyptian Calendar Navbar}} [[Category:Months of the Ancient Egyptian Calendars]] [[ru:Ренутет]] {{wikidata link|Q132171870}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

'''Stelae found in granaries and field boundaries''' provide evidence of this cult. In the '''Theban calendar''', this corresponded to '''{{ill|Pa-en-Chonsu|1=Pa-en-Chonsu|de|Pa-en-Chonsu|1=Pa-en-Chonsu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pa-en-Chonsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Pa-en-Chonsu|qq=|WD=Q56414619}} 1 (Pachon 1).''' Additionally, as a '''fertility goddess''', Renenutet was credited with ensuring the '''"emergence of flax."''' Because flax was processed into linen, she also bore the titles '''"Lady of the Garments"''' and '''"Overseer of the Temple's Clothing Chamber,"''' referring to the belief that gods were clothed in '''"the garment of Renenutet."''' Records from the '''{{ill|Solar Temple of Niuserre|de|Sonnenheiligtum des Niuserre|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Solar Temple of Niuserre|qq=|WD=Q2009417}}''' indicate that the '''barley harvest''' took place in '''February and March.'''{{ill|Elmar Edel|de|Elmar Edel|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Elmar Edel|qq=|WD=Q105633}}: ''Zu den Inschriften auf den Jahreszeitenreliefs der „Weltkammer“ aus dem Sonnenheiligtum des Niuserre, Teil 2.'' Vandenhoeck & Ruprecht, Göttingen 1964, p. 200. == Name Change == The '''first Shemu month''' was later renamed '''{{ill|Pa-en-Chonsu|1=Pa-en-Chonsu|de|Pa-en-Chonsu|1=Pa-en-Chonsu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pa-en-Chonsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Pa-en-Chonsu|qq=|WD=Q56414619}} (Pachon).''' == See Also == * {{ill|Tebtunis lunar calendar|de|Tebtunis-Mondkalender|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tebtunis lunar calendar|qq=|WD=Q1458800}} == References == * {{ill|Rolf Krauss|de|Rolf Krauss|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rolf Krauss|qq=|WD=Q91239}}: ''Sothis- und Monddaten: Studien zur astronomischen und technischen Chronologie Altägyptens.'' In the '''Theban calendar''', this corresponded to '''{{ill|Pa-en-Chonsu|1=Pa-en-Chonsu|de|Pa-en-Chonsu|1=Pa-en-Chonsu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pa-en-Chonsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Pa-en-Chonsu|qq=|WD=Q56414619}} 1 (Pachon 1).''' Additionally, as a '''fertility goddess''', Renenutet was credited with ensuring the '''"emergence of flax."''' Because flax was processed into linen, she also bore the titles '''"Lady of the Garments"''' and '''"Overseer of the Temple's Clothing Chamber,"''' referring to the belief that gods were clothed in '''"the garment of Renenutet."''' Records from the '''{{ill|Solar Temple of Niuserre|de|Sonnenheiligtum des Niuserre|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Solar Temple of Niuserre|qq=|WD=Q2009417}}''' indicate that the '''barley harvest''' took place in '''February and March.'''{{ill|Elmar Edel|de|Elmar Edel|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Elmar Edel|qq=|WD=Q105633}}: ''Zu den Inschriften auf den Jahreszeitenreliefs der „Weltkammer“ aus dem Sonnenheiligtum des Niuserre, Teil 2.'' In: ''Nachrichten der Akademie der Wissenschaften in Göttingen.'' No. 5. Vandenhoeck & Ruprecht, Göttingen 1964, p. 200. == Name Change == The '''first Shemu month''' was later renamed '''{{ill|Pa-en-Chonsu|1=Pa-en-Chonsu|de|Pa-en-Chonsu|1=Pa-en-Chonsu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pa-en-Chonsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Pa-en-Chonsu|qq=|WD=Q56414619}} (Pachon).''' == See Also == * {{ill|Tebtunis lunar calendar|de|Tebtunis-Mondkalender|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tebtunis lunar calendar|qq=|WD=Q1458800}} == References == * {{ill|Rolf Krauss|de|Rolf Krauss|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rolf Krauss|qq=|WD=Q91239}}: ''Sothis- und Monddaten: Studien zur astronomischen und technischen Chronologie Altägyptens.'' Gerstenberg, Hildesheim 1985, {{Falsche ISBN|3-8067-8086-X}}. * [[:en:Richard Anthony Parker|Richard Anthony Parker]]: ''The calendars of ancient Egypt.'' Chicago Press, Chicago 1950. * {{ill|Siegfried Schott|de|Siegfried Schott|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Siegfried Schott|qq=|WD=Q102228}}: ''Altägyptische Festdaten.'' Verlag der Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur, Mainz/ Wiesbaden 1950. == Notes == {{Navigationsleiste Ägyptische Kalendermonate bis zum Anfang des Neuen Reiches}} {{Ancient Egyptian Lunar Months}} {{translated page|de|Renutet|version=|comment=Imported full de history, revisions duplicated and version not here due to an error with my bot related to the German being under the same pagename as the english}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Months of the Ancient Egyptian Calendars]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian festivals]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]][[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Harvest festivals]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[de:Renutet]] [[ru:%D0%A0%D0%B5%D0%BD%D1%83%D1%82%D0%B5%D1%82]] {{Egyptian Calendar Navbar}} [[Category:Months of the Ancient Egyptian Calendars]] [[ru:Ренутет]] {{wikidata link|Q132171870}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

[[de:Renutet]][[ru:Ренутет]] [[de:Renutet]][[ru:Ренутет]] [[de:Renutet]][[ru:Ренутет]] {{Infobox hieroglyphs |NAME = r:n-Z7-t:t |NAME-TRANSKRIPTION = |NAME-ERKLÄRUNG = [[:en:Renenutet|Renenutet]]
''Rnwtt''
''The Nourishing One'' |NAME2 = N11:Z1 S-N35B
1-nu-schemu |NAME2-TRANSKRIPTION = ''1-nw-šmw'' |NAME2-ERKLÄRUNG = ''First month of the [[:en:Season of the Harvest|Schemu]] period'' |NAME3 = F13:X1-G17-G1-O34:Aa1-M17-M17:X1-M33
Wepet-em-asech-ati |NAME3-TRANSKRIPTION = ''Wpt-m-3sḫ-jtj'' |NAME3-ERKLÄRUNG = ''Beginning of the [[:en:Barley|barley]] harvest'' |IMAGE1 = Illustration Hordeum vulgare0.jpg |IMAGE1-WIDTH = 210px }} '''Renutet''' ("The Nourishing One") denoted in the [[:en:Egyptian calendar|Ancient Egyptian calendar]] the beginning of the harvest season and the first month of the [[:en:Season of the Harvest|Schemu]] period as the "nourishing month." From the {{ill|Predynastic Egypt|de|Prädynastik (Ägypten)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Predynastic Egypt|qq=|WD=Q714601}} until the end of the [[:en:Middle Kingdom of Egypt|Middle Kingdom of Egypt]], Renutet, originally the ninth month of the {{ill|Sothic calendar|1=Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar|de|Mondkalender (Altes Ägypten)|1=Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar#Der ursprüngliche Mondkalender|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sothic calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar|qq=|WD=Q1944246}}, corresponded to the period from early [[:en:February|February]] to early [[:en:March|March]]. == History == [[:en:Alan Gardiner|Alan Gardiner]] and [[:en:Richard Anthony Parker|Richard Anthony Parker]] theorized that Renutet underwent a calendrical shift over time, eventually moving to the eighth month by the [[:en:New Kingdom of Egypt|New Kingdom of Egypt]]. In the {{ill|Ebers calendar|de|Ebers-Kalender|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebers calendar|qq=|WD=Q1279717}}, dated to around '''1517 BCE''', Renutet corresponded to the third [[:en:Season of the Emergence|Peret]] month, spanning from '''February 14 to March 15''' ([[:en:Elephantine|Elephantine]]) and '''February 19 to March 20''' ([[:en:Memphis, Egypt|Memphis (Egypt)]]). == Significance == Renutet, identified with the goddess [[:en:Renenutet|Renenutet]], was revered as: * '''"Lady of the Granary"''' * '''"Lady of the Fertile Land"''' * '''"Lady of the Harvest Blessing"''' During the harvest, the Egyptians made offerings to her. '''Stelae found in granaries and field boundaries''' provide evidence of this cult. The '''{{ill|Renenutet Festival|de|Renenutet-Fest|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Renenutet Festival|qq=|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}}''' was later celebrated on '''{{ill|Pa-en-Renenutet|lt=Pharmouthi|de|Pharmouthi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pa-en-Renenutet|qq=|WD=Q846942}} 27''', known as the '''"Day of Measuring the [[:en:Barley|barley]]."''' In the '''Theban calendar''', this corresponded to '''{{ill|Pa-en-Chonsu|1=Pa-en-Chonsu|de|Pa-en-Chonsu|1=Pa-en-Chonsu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pa-en-Chonsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Pa-en-Chonsu|qq=|WD=Q56414619}} 1 (Pachon 1).''' Additionally, as a '''fertility goddess''', Renenutet was credited with ensuring the '''"emergence of flax."''' Because flax was processed into linen, she also bore the titles '''"Lady of the Garments"''' and '''"Overseer of the Temple's Clothing Chamber,"''' referring to the belief that gods were clothed in '''"the garment of Renenutet."''' Records from the '''{{ill|Solar Temple of Niuserre|de|Sonnenheiligtum des Niuserre|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Solar Temple of Niuserre|qq=|WD=Q2009417}}''' indicate that the '''barley harvest''' took place in '''February and March.'''{{ill|Elmar Edel|de|Elmar Edel|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Elmar Edel|qq=|WD=Q105633}}: ''Zu den Inschriften auf den Jahreszeitenreliefs der „Weltkammer“ aus dem Sonnenheiligtum des Niuserre, Teil 2.'' In: ''Nachrichten der Akademie der Wissenschaften in Göttingen.'' No. 5. Vandenhoeck & Ruprecht, Göttingen 1964, p. 200. == Name Change == The '''first Shemu month''' was later renamed '''{{ill|Pa-en-Chonsu|1=Pa-en-Chonsu|de|Pa-en-Chonsu|1=Pa-en-Chonsu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pa-en-Chonsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Pa-en-Chonsu|qq=|WD=Q56414619}} (Pachon).''' == See Also == * {{ill|Tebtunis lunar calendar|de|Tebtunis-Mondkalender|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tebtunis lunar calendar|qq=|WD=Q1458800}} == References == * {{ill|Rolf Krauss|de|Rolf Krauss|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rolf Krauss|qq=|WD=Q91239}}: ''Sothis- und Monddaten: Studien zur astronomischen und technischen Chronologie Altägyptens.'' Gerstenberg, Hildesheim 1985, {{Falsche ISBN|3-8067-8086-X}}. * [[:en:Richard Anthony Parker|Richard Anthony Parker]]: ''The calendars of ancient Egypt.'' Chicago Press, Chicago 1950. * {{ill|Siegfried Schott|de|Siegfried Schott|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Siegfried Schott|qq=|WD=Q102228}}: ''Altägyptische Festdaten.'' Verlag der Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur, Mainz/ Wiesbaden 1950. == Notes == {{Navigationsleiste Ägyptische Kalendermonate bis zum Anfang des Neuen Reiches}} {{Ancient Egyptian Lunar Months}} {{translated page|de|Renutet|version=|comment=Imported full de history, revisions duplicated and version not here due to an error with my bot related to the German being under the same pagename as the english}} {{draft categories| }} [[de:Renutet]] [[ru:%D0%A0%D0%B5%D0%BD%D1%83%D1%82%D0%B5%D1%82]] {{Egyptian Calendar Navbar}} [[ru:Ренутет]] {{wikidata link|Q132171870}} [[Category:Ancient Egyptian festivals]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Harvest festivals]] [[Category:Months of the Ancient Egyptian Calendars]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]]

Wepet-em-asech-ati |NAME3-TRANSKRIPTION = ''Wpt-m-3sḫ-jtj'' |NAME3-ERKLÄRUNG = ''Beginning of the [[:en:Barley|barley]] harvest'' |IMAGE1 = Illustration Hordeum vulgare0.jpg |IMAGE1-WIDTH = 210px }} '''Renutet''' ("The Nourishing One") denoted in the [[:en:Egyptian calendar|Ancient Egyptian calendar]] the beginning of the harvest season and the first month of the [[:en:Season of the Harvest|Schemu]] period as the "nourishing month." From the {{ill|Predynastic Egypt|de|Prädynastik (Ägypten)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Predynastic Egypt|qq=|WD=Q714601}} until the end of the [[:en:Middle Kingdom of Egypt|Middle Kingdom of Egypt]], Renutet, originally the ninth month of the {{ill|Sothic calendar|1=Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar|de|Mondkalender (Altes Ägypten)|1=Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar#Der ursprüngliche Mondkalender|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sothic calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar|qq=|WD=Q1944246}}, corresponded to the period from early [[:en:February|February]] to early [[:en:March|March]]. == History == [[:en:Alan Gardiner|Alan Gardiner]] and [[:en:Richard Anthony Parker|Richard Anthony Parker]] theorized that Renutet underwent a calendrical shift over time, eventually moving to the eighth month by the [[:en:New Kingdom of Egypt|New Kingdom of Egypt]]. In the {{ill|Ebers calendar|de|Ebers-Kalender|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebers calendar|qq=|WD=Q1279717}}, dated to around '''1517 BCE''', Renutet corresponded to the third [[:en:Season of the Emergence|Peret]] month, spanning from '''February 14 to March 15''' ([[:en:Elephantine|Elephantine]]) and '''February 19 to March 20''' ([[:en:Memphis, Egypt|Memphis (Egypt)]]). == Significance == Renutet, identified with the goddess [[:en:Renenutet|Renenutet]], was revered as: * '''"Lady of the Granary"''' * '''"Lady of the Fertile Land"''' * '''"Lady of the Harvest Blessing"''' During the harvest, the Egyptians made offerings to her. '''Stelae found in granaries and field boundaries''' provide evidence of this cult. The '''{{ill|Renenutet Festival|de|Renenutet-Fest|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Renenutet Festival|qq=|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}}''' was later celebrated on '''{{ill|Pa-en-Renenutet|lt=Pharmouthi|de|Pharmouthi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pa-en-Renenutet|qq=|WD=Q846942}} 27''', known as the '''"Day of Measuring the [[:en:Barley|barley]]."''' In the '''Theban calendar''', this corresponded to '''{{ill|Pa-en-Chonsu|1=Pa-en-Chonsu|de|Pa-en-Chonsu|1=Pa-en-Chonsu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pa-en-Chonsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Pa-en-Chonsu|qq=|WD=Q56414619}} 1 (Pachon 1).''' Additionally, as a '''fertility goddess''', Renenutet was credited with ensuring the '''"emergence of flax."''' Because flax was processed into linen, she also bore the titles '''"Lady of the Garments"''' and '''"Overseer of the Temple's Clothing Chamber,"''' referring to the belief that gods were clothed in '''"the garment of Renenutet."''' Records from the '''{{ill|Solar Temple of Niuserre|de|Sonnenheiligtum des Niuserre|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Solar Temple of Niuserre|qq=|WD=Q2009417}}''' indicate that the '''barley harvest''' took place in '''February and March.'''{{ill|Elmar Edel|de|Elmar Edel|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Elmar Edel|qq=|WD=Q105633}}: ''Zu den Inschriften auf den Jahreszeitenreliefs der „Weltkammer“ aus dem Sonnenheiligtum des Niuserre, Teil 2.'' In: ''Nachrichten der Akademie der Wissenschaften in Göttingen.'' No. 5. Vandenhoeck & Ruprecht, Göttingen 1964, p. 200. == Name Change == The '''first Shemu month''' was later renamed '''{{ill|Pa-en-Chonsu|1=Pa-en-Chonsu|de|Pa-en-Chonsu|1=Pa-en-Chonsu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pa-en-Chonsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Pa-en-Chonsu|qq=|WD=Q56414619}} (Pachon).''' == See Also == * {{ill|Tebtunis lunar calendar|de|Tebtunis-Mondkalender|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tebtunis lunar calendar|qq=|WD=Q1458800}} == References == * {{ill|Rolf Krauss|de|Rolf Krauss|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rolf Krauss|qq=|WD=Q91239}}: ''Sothis- und Monddaten: Studien zur astronomischen und technischen Chronologie Altägyptens.'' Gerstenberg, Hildesheim 1985, {{Falsche ISBN|3-8067-8086-X}}. * [[:en:Richard Anthony Parker|Richard Anthony Parker]]: ''The calendars of ancient Egypt.'' Chicago Press, Chicago 1950. * {{ill|Siegfried Schott|de|Siegfried Schott|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Siegfried Schott|qq=|WD=Q102228}}: ''Altägyptische Festdaten.'' Verlag der Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur, Mainz/ Wiesbaden 1950. == Notes == {{Navigationsleiste Ägyptische Kalendermonate bis zum Anfang des Neuen Reiches}} {{Ancient Egyptian Lunar Months}} {{translated page|de|Renutet|version=|comment=Imported full de history, revisions duplicated and version not here due to an error with my bot related to the German being under the same pagename as the english}} [[de:Renutet]] [[ru:%D0%A0%D0%B5%D0%BD%D1%83%D1%82%D0%B5%D1%82]] {{Egyptian Calendar Navbar}} [[ru:Ренутет]] {{wikidata link|Q132171870}} [[Category:Ancient Egyptian festivals]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Harvest festivals]] [[Category:Months of the Ancient Egyptian Calendars]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]]

1-nu-schemu |NAME2-TRANSKRIPTION = ''1-nw-šmw'' |NAME2-ERKLÄRUNG = ''First month of the {{ill|Season of the Harvest|en|Season of the Harvest|WD=Q1248622|lt=Schemu}} period'' |NAME3 = F13:X1-G17-G1-O34:Aa1-M17-M17:X1-M33
Wepet-em-asech-ati |NAME3-TRANSKRIPTION = ''Wpt-m-3sḫ-jtj'' |NAME3-ERKLÄRUNG = ''Beginning of the {{ill|Barley|en|Barley|WD=Q11577|lt=barley}} harvest'' |IMAGE1 = Illustration Hordeum vulgare0.jpg |IMAGE1-WIDTH = 210px }} '''Renutet''' ("The Nourishing One") denoted in the {{ill|Egyptian calendar|en|Egyptian calendar|WD=Q254101|lt=Ancient Egyptian calendar}} the beginning of the harvest season and the first month of the {{ill|Season of the Harvest|en|Season of the Harvest|WD=Q1248622|lt=Schemu}} period as the "nourishing month." From the {{ill|Predynastic Egypt|de|Prädynastik (Ägypten)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Predynastic Egypt|qq=|WD=Q714601}} until the end of the {{ill|Middle Kingdom of Egypt|en|Middle Kingdom of Egypt|WD=Q191324|lt=Middle Kingdom of Egypt}}, Renutet, originally the ninth month of the {{ill|Sothic calendar|1=Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar|de|Mondkalender (Altes Ägypten)|1=Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar#Der ursprüngliche Mondkalender|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sothic calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar|qq=|WD=Q1944246}}, corresponded to the period from early {{ill|February|en|February|WD=Q109|lt=February}} to early {{ill|March|en|March|WD=Q110|lt=March}}. == History == {{ill|Alan Gardiner|en|Alan Gardiner|WD=Q441060|lt=Alan Gardiner}} and {{ill|Richard Anthony Parker|en|Richard Anthony Parker|WD=Q2148992|lt=Richard Anthony Parker}} theorized that Renutet underwent a calendrical shift over time, eventually moving to the eighth month by the {{ill|New Kingdom of Egypt|en|New Kingdom of Egypt|WD=Q180568|lt=New Kingdom of Egypt}}. In the {{ill|Ebers calendar|de|Ebers-Kalender|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebers calendar|qq=|WD=Q1279717}}, dated to around '''1517 BCE''', Renutet corresponded to the third {{ill|Season of the Emergence|en|Season of the Emergence|WD=Q2037143|lt=Peret}} month, spanning from '''February 14 to March 15''' ({{ill|Elephantine|en|Elephantine|WD=Q284009|lt=Elephantine}}) and '''February 19 to March 20''' ({{ill|Memphis, Egypt|en|Memphis, Egypt|WD=Q5715|lt=Memphis (Egypt)}}). == Significance == Renutet, identified with the goddess {{ill|Renenutet|en|Renenutet|WD=Q922680|lt=Renenutet}}, was revered as: * '''"Lady of the Granary"''' * '''"Lady of the Fertile Land"''' * '''"Lady of the Harvest Blessing"''' During the harvest, the Egyptians made offerings to her. '''Stelae found in granaries and field boundaries''' provide evidence of this cult. The '''{{ill|Renenutet Festival|de|Renenutet-Fest|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Renenutet Festival|qq=|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}}''' was later celebrated on '''{{ill|Pa-en-Renenutet|lt=Pharmouthi|de|Pharmouthi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pa-en-Renenutet|qq=|WD=Q846942}} 27''', known as the '''"Day of Measuring the {{ill|Barley|en|Barley|WD=Q11577|lt=barley}}."''' In the '''Theban calendar''', this corresponded to '''{{ill|Pa-en-Chonsu|1=Pa-en-Chonsu|de|Pa-en-Chonsu|1=Pa-en-Chonsu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pa-en-Chonsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Pa-en-Chonsu|qq=|WD=Q56414619}} 1 (Pachon 1).''' Additionally, as a '''fertility goddess''', Renenutet was credited with ensuring the '''"emergence of flax."''' Because flax was processed into linen, she also bore the titles '''"Lady of the Garments"''' and '''"Overseer of the Temple's Clothing Chamber,"''' referring to the belief that gods were clothed in '''"the garment of Renenutet."''' Records from the '''{{ill|Solar Temple of Niuserre|de|Sonnenheiligtum des Niuserre|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Solar Temple of Niuserre|qq=|WD=Q2009417}}''' indicate that the '''barley harvest''' took place in '''February and March.'''{{ill|Elmar Edel|de|Elmar Edel|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Elmar Edel|qq=|WD=Q105633}}: ''Zu den Inschriften auf den Jahreszeitenreliefs der „Weltkammer“ aus dem Sonnenheiligtum des Niuserre, Teil 2.'' In: ''Nachrichten der Akademie der Wissenschaften in Göttingen.'' No. 5. Vandenhoeck & Ruprecht, Göttingen 1964, p. 200. == Name Change == The '''first Shemu month''' was later renamed '''{{ill|Pa-en-Chonsu|1=Pa-en-Chonsu|de|Pa-en-Chonsu|1=Pa-en-Chonsu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pa-en-Chonsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Pa-en-Chonsu|qq=|WD=Q56414619}} (Pachon).''' == See Also == * {{ill|Tebtunis lunar calendar|de|Tebtunis-Mondkalender|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tebtunis lunar calendar|qq=|WD=Q1458800}} == References == * {{ill|Rolf Krauss|de|Rolf Krauss|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rolf Krauss|qq=|WD=Q91239}}: ''Sothis- und Monddaten: Studien zur astronomischen und technischen Chronologie Altägyptens.'' Records from the '''{{ill|Solar Temple of Niuserre|de|Sonnenheiligtum des Niuserre|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Solar Temple of Niuserre|qq=|WD=Q2009417|1=Solar Temple of Niuserre}}''' indicate that the '''barley harvest''' took place in '''February and March.'''{{ill|Elmar Edel|de|Elmar Edel|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Elmar Edel|qq=|WD=Q105633|1=Elmar Edel}}: ''Zu den Inschriften auf den Jahreszeitenreliefs der „Weltkammer“ aus dem Sonnenheiligtum des Niuserre, Teil 2.'' In: ''Nachrichten der Akademie der Wissenschaften in Göttingen.'' No. 5. Vandenhoeck & Ruprecht, Göttingen 1964, p. 200. == Name Change == The '''first Shemu month''' was later renamed '''{{ill|Pa-en-Chonsu|1=Pa-en-Chonsu|de|Pa-en-Chonsu|1=Pa-en-Chonsu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pa-en-Chonsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Pa-en-Chonsu|qq=|WD=Q56414619}} (Pachon).''' == See Also == * {{ill|Tebtunis lunar calendar|de|Tebtunis-Mondkalender|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tebtunis lunar calendar|qq=|WD=Q1458800|1=Tebtunis lunar calendar}} == References == * {{ill|Rolf Krauss|de|Rolf Krauss|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rolf Krauss|qq=|WD=Q91239|1=Rolf Krauss}}: ''Sothis- und Monddaten: Studien zur astronomischen und technischen Chronologie Altägyptens.'' Gerstenberg, Hildesheim 1985, {{Falsche ISBN|3-8067-8086-X}}. * {{ill|Richard Anthony Parker|en|Richard Anthony Parker|WD=Q2148992|lt=Richard Anthony Parker|1=Richard Anthony Parker}}: ''The calendars of ancient Egypt.'' Chicago Press, Chicago 1950. * {{ill|Siegfried Schott|de|Siegfried Schott|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Siegfried Schott|qq=|WD=Q102228|1=Siegfried Schott}}: ''Altägyptische Festdaten.'' Verlag der Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur, Mainz/ Wiesbaden 1950. == Notes == {{Navigationsleiste Ägyptische Kalendermonate bis zum Anfang des Neuen Reiches}} {{Ancient Egyptian Lunar Months}} {{translated page|de|Renutet|version=|comment=Imported full de history, revisions duplicated and version not here due to an error with my bot related to the German being under the same pagename as the english}} [[de:Renutet]] [[ru:%D0%A0%D0%B5%D0%BD%D1%83%D1%82%D0%B5%D1%82]] {{Egyptian Calendar Navbar}} [[ru:Ренутет]] {{wikidata link|Q132171870}} [[Category:Ancient Egyptian festivals]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Harvest festivals]] [[Category:Months of the Ancient Egyptian Calendars]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:pages]]

[[de:Renutet]][[ru:Ренутет]] [[de:Renutet]][[ru:Ренутет]] [[de:Renutet]][[ru:Ренутет]] {{Infobox hieroglyphs |NAME = r:n-Z7-t:t |NAME-TRANSKRIPTION = |NAME-ERKLÄRUNG = {{ill|Renenutet|en|Renenutet|WD=Q922680|lt=Renenutet|1=Renenutet}}
''Rnwtt''
''The Nourishing One'' |NAME2 = N11:Z1 S-N35B
1-nu-schemu |NAME2-TRANSKRIPTION = ''1-nw-šmw'' |NAME2-ERKLÄRUNG = ''First month of the {{ill|Season of the Harvest|en|Season of the Harvest|WD=Q1248622|lt=Schemu|1=Season of the Harvest}} period'' |NAME3 = F13:X1-G17-G1-O34:Aa1-M17-M17:X1-M33
Wepet-em-asech-ati |NAME3-TRANSKRIPTION = ''Wpt-m-3sḫ-jtj'' |NAME3-ERKLÄRUNG = ''Beginning of the {{ill|Barley|en|Barley|WD=Q11577|lt=barley|1=Barley}} harvest'' |IMAGE1 = Illustration Hordeum vulgare0.jpg |IMAGE1-WIDTH = 210px }} '''Renutet''' ("The Nourishing One") denoted in the {{ill|Egyptian calendar|en|Egyptian calendar|WD=Q254101|lt=Ancient Egyptian calendar|1=Egyptian calendar}} the beginning of the harvest season and the first month of the {{ill|Season of the Harvest|en|Season of the Harvest|WD=Q1248622|lt=Schemu|1=Season of the Harvest}} period as the "nourishing month." From the {{ill|Predynastic Egypt|de|Prädynastik (Ägypten)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Predynastic Egypt|qq=|WD=Q714601|1=Predynastic Egypt}} until the end of the {{ill|Middle Kingdom of Egypt|en|Middle Kingdom of Egypt|WD=Q191324|lt=Middle Kingdom of Egypt|1=Middle Kingdom of Egypt}}, Renutet, originally the ninth month of the {{ill|Sothic calendar|1=Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar|de|Mondkalender (Altes Ägypten)|1=Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar#Der ursprüngliche Mondkalender|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sothic calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar|qq=|WD=Q1944246}}, corresponded to the period from early {{ill|February|en|February|WD=Q109|lt=February|1=February}} to early {{ill|March|en|March|WD=Q110|lt=March|1=March}}. == History == {{ill|Alan Gardiner|en|Alan Gardiner|WD=Q441060|lt=Alan Gardiner|1=Alan Gardiner}} and {{ill|Richard Anthony Parker|en|Richard Anthony Parker|WD=Q2148992|lt=Richard Anthony Parker|1=Richard Anthony Parker}} theorized that Renutet underwent a calendrical shift over time, eventually moving to the eighth month by the {{ill|New Kingdom of Egypt|en|New Kingdom of Egypt|WD=Q180568|lt=New Kingdom of Egypt|1=New Kingdom of Egypt}}. In the {{ill|Ebers calendar|de|Ebers-Kalender|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebers calendar|qq=|WD=Q1279717|1=Ebers calendar}}, dated to around '''1517 BCE''', Renutet corresponded to the third {{ill|Season of the Emergence|en|Season of the Emergence|WD=Q2037143|lt=Peret|1=Season of the Emergence}} month, spanning from '''February 14 to March 15''' ({{ill|Elephantine|en|Elephantine|WD=Q284009|lt=Elephantine|1=Elephantine}}) and '''February 19 to March 20''' ({{ill|Memphis, Egypt|en|Memphis, Egypt|WD=Q5715|lt=Memphis (Egypt)|1=Memphis, Egypt}}). == Significance == Renutet, identified with the goddess {{ill|Renenutet|en|Renenutet|WD=Q922680|lt=Renenutet|1=Renenutet}}, was revered as: * '''"Lady of the Granary"''' * '''"Lady of the Fertile Land"''' * '''"Lady of the Harvest Blessing"''' During the harvest, the Egyptians made offerings to her. '''Stelae found in granaries and field boundaries''' provide evidence of this cult. The '''{{ill|Renenutet Festival|de|Renenutet-Fest|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Renenutet Festival|qq=|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}}''' was later celebrated on '''{{ill|Pa-en-Renenutet|lt=Pharmouthi|de|Pharmouthi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pa-en-Renenutet|qq=|WD=Q846942|1=Pa-en-Renenutet}} 27''', known as the '''"Day of Measuring the {{ill|Barley|en|Barley|WD=Q11577|lt=barley|1=Barley}}."''' In the '''Theban calendar''', this corresponded to '''{{ill|Pa-en-Chonsu|1=Pa-en-Chonsu|de|Pa-en-Chonsu|1=Pa-en-Chonsu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pa-en-Chonsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Pa-en-Chonsu|qq=|WD=Q56414619}} 1 (Pachon 1).''' Additionally, as a '''fertility goddess''', Renenutet was credited with ensuring the '''"emergence of flax."''' Because flax was processed into linen, she also bore the titles '''"Lady of the Garments"''' and '''"Overseer of the Temple's Clothing Chamber,"''' referring to the belief that gods were clothed in '''"the garment of Renenutet."''' Records from the '''{{ill|Solar Temple of Niuserre|de|Sonnenheiligtum des Niuserre|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Solar Temple of Niuserre|qq=|WD=Q2009417|1=Solar Temple of Niuserre}}''' indicate that the '''barley harvest''' took place in '''February and March.'''{{ill|Elmar Edel|de|Elmar Edel|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Elmar Edel|qq=|WD=Q105633|1=Elmar Edel}}: ''Zu den Inschriften auf den Jahreszeitenreliefs der „Weltkammer“ aus dem Sonnenheiligtum des Niuserre, Teil 2.'' In: ''Nachrichten der Akademie der Wissenschaften in Göttingen.'' No. 5. Vandenhoeck & Ruprecht, Göttingen 1964, p. 200. == Name Change == The '''first Shemu month''' was later renamed '''{{ill|Pa-en-Chonsu|1=Pa-en-Chonsu|de|Pa-en-Chonsu|1=Pa-en-Chonsu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pa-en-Chonsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Pa-en-Chonsu|qq=|WD=Q56414619}} (Pachon).''' == See Also == * {{ill|Tebtunis lunar calendar|de|Tebtunis-Mondkalender|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tebtunis lunar calendar|qq=|WD=Q1458800|1=Tebtunis lunar calendar}} == References == * {{ill|Rolf Krauss|de|Rolf Krauss|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rolf Krauss|qq=|WD=Q91239|1=Rolf Krauss}}: ''Sothis- und Monddaten: Studien zur astronomischen und technischen Chronologie Altägyptens.'' Gerstenberg, Hildesheim 1985, {{Falsche ISBN|3-8067-8086-X}}. * {{ill|Richard Anthony Parker|en|Richard Anthony Parker|WD=Q2148992|lt=Richard Anthony Parker|1=Richard Anthony Parker}}: ''The calendars of ancient Egypt.'' Chicago Press, Chicago 1950. * {{ill|Siegfried Schott|de|Siegfried Schott|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Siegfried Schott|qq=|WD=Q102228|1=Siegfried Schott}}: ''Altägyptische Festdaten.'' Verlag der Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur, Mainz/ Wiesbaden 1950. == Notes == {{Navigationsleiste Ägyptische Kalendermonate bis zum Anfang des Neuen Reiches}} {{Ancient Egyptian Lunar Months}} {{translated page|de|Renutet|version=|comment=Imported full de history, revisions duplicated and version not here due to an error with my bot related to the German being under the same pagename as the english}} [[de:Renutet]] [[ru:%D0%A0%D0%B5%D0%BD%D1%83%D1%82%D0%B5%D1%82]] {{Egyptian Calendar Navbar}} [[ru:Ренутет]] {{wikidata link|Q132171870}} [[Category:Ancient Egyptian festivals]] [[Category:Harvest festivals]] [[Category:Months of the Ancient Egyptian Calendars]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:pages]]
